|

Peter,
Thanksgiving is tough for me. It was the first holiday after I lost both of my parents when I was 36, one of the hardest times of my life. I miss them every day.
But I am so grateful for the time I had with them--for the values they taught me, for the faith they gave me, and for the love that still carries me forward.
I am grateful to be running for Congress in my home district to make life affordable again for middle-class families like mine. For care and food. For energy and education. For the dignity of the people who make those things possible. For good jobs that let folks spend more time with the ones they love.
In other words, this Thanksgiving, I'm not just thankful for the food on the table--I'm thankful for the hands that put it there.
Farmers. Energy and infrastructure workers. Public servants who hold our communities together.
I also want to send a heartfelt thank you to you.
I know times are tough right now. I know how hard you work for every paycheck. The fact that you have chosen to pitch in your hard-earned money to support this grassroots campaign is something I will never take for granted. It is an honor to have you in this fight.
Today, I hope you can tune out the noise, hold your loved ones close, and take a moment to rest. You've earned it.
Happy Thanksgiving,
Trisha Calvarese Democrat for CO-04
---

name: George Moraitis for Congress
subject: Happy Thanksgiving
disclaimer_text: Paid for by George Moraitis for Congress Committee
body:
| | |
---
| | [](https://km78svhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001TFDRllbDV7WkkmY7M06jkOOD7j_aKgXv7noqop75aFuqha0iHffANZUNhHfVD7idxy5sMDsdu-jA3rffi6Irfc3lMz7Ty8eQZrfTZGKvTQS0hnxUrwAozQGxCfV2mlhcZyK5fsKuEDTakEjxwBEzd7DkfG4Ud1J2&c=Nq_ZQUDv_kzh5Z88PJ_-KUcQWFYvNa19NQYzBgI71A0iaZorRXp63w==&ch=7jNXWdLG13ugnxoYWSy3c7C0AxntS1yZd3847-pgDZEqMPFm35hneg==)
---
---
|
# AMERICA FIRST. NAVY VETERAN.
# PROVEN LEADER.
---
| |
---
---
| Dear Peter,
As we gather with family and friends this Thanksgiving, I am reminded of the Pilgrims who left England for Holland in 1608, before coming to America in 1620. A year later they celebrated the first Thanksgiving with the Native Americans who helped them survive. The Pilgrims came to this land for the opportunity to worship freely according to their beliefs, to own land, and to earn a livelihood in the New World.
Our nation was later founded on these ideals of self-government, free enterprise markets, property rights, and freedom of religion. The same liberties that allowed the Pilgrims to flourish 400 years ago, were later fought for in the American Revolution when we issued the Declaration of Independence, and eventually codified in the Bill of Rights to our Constitution. Each generation of Americans must protect and fight for these sacred liberties.
I hope that you find inspiration for your celebration, from the words penned by Abraham Lincoln in his 1864 Thanksgiving Day Proclamation, to a nation weary after nearly 4 years of civil war:
"Now, therefore, I, Abraham Lincoln, President of the United States, do hereby appoint and set apart the last Thursday in November next as a day which I desire to be observed by all my fellow-citizens, wherever they may then be, as a day of thanksgiving and praise to Almighty God, the beneficent Creator and Ruler of the Universe. And I do further recommend to my fellow-citizens aforesaid that on that occasion they do reverently humble themselves in the dust and from thence offer up penitent and fervent prayers and supplications to the Great Disposer of Events for a return of the inestimable blessings of peace, union, and harmony throughout the land which it has pleased Him to assign as a dwelling place for ourselves and for our posterity throughout all generations." Proclamation 118--Thanksgiving Day, 1864
I pray that as you celebrate Thanksgiving today, you join me in thanking God for our great nation, for those with the courage to found it and fight for it, and for those who continue to serve and protect us today. God bless you and your family as you celebrate Thanksgiving!
Sincerely,
---
|
---
| George
---

clean_body: | | | | | == | AMERICA FIRST. NAVY VETERAN. PROVEN LEADER. | | | Dear Peter, As we gather with family and friends this Thanksgiving, I am reminded of the Pilgrims who left England for Holland in 1608, before coming to America in 1620. A year later they celebrated the first Thanksgiving with the Native Americans who helped them survive. The Pilgrims came to this land for the opportunity to worship freely according to their beliefs, to own land, and to earn a livelihood in the New World. Our nation was later founded on these ideals of self-government, free enterprise markets, property rights, and freedom of religion. The same liberties that allowed the Pilgrims to flourish 400 years ago, were later fought for in the American Revolution when we issued the Declaration of Independence, and eventually codified in the Bill of Rights to our Constitution. Each generation of Americans must protect and fight for these sacred liberties. I hope that you find inspiration for your celebration, from the words penned by Abraham Lincoln in his 1864 Thanksgiving Day Proclamation, to a nation weary after nearly 4 years of civil war: "Now, therefore, I, Abraham Lincoln, President of the United States, do hereby appoint and set apart the last Thursday in November next as a day which I desire to be observed by all my fellow-citizens, wherever they may then be, as a day of thanksgiving and praise to Almighty God, the beneficent Creator and Ruler of the Universe. And I do further recommend to my fellow-citizens aforesaid that on that occasion they do reverently humble themselves in the dust and from thence offer up penitent and fervent prayers and supplications to the Great Disposer of Events for a return of the inestimable blessings of peace, union, and harmony throughout the land which it has pleased Him to assign as a dwelling place for ourselves and for our posterity throughout all generations." Proclamation 118--Thanksgiving Day, 1864 I pray that as you celebrate Thanksgiving today, you join me in thanking God for our great nation, for those with the courage to found it and fight for it, and for those who continue to serve and protect us today. God bless you and your family as you celebrate Thanksgiving! Sincerely, | | George | | == | == ---|--- == | == ---|--- == | == ---|--- | FOLLOW US ON SOCIAL MEDIA FOR UPDATES! | == == == == mailto:info@george4congress.com | | == | == | == | == | == | == | | | | == | Paid for by George Moraitis for Congress Committee | | | | George Moraitis for Congress | 1103 Hays Street | Tallahassee, FL 32301 US | | | | | | |

name: Erika Kirk
subject: Thank you, Peter
body:
This Thanksgiving, I hope you hold your loved ones a little closer. Cherish the moments. Speak life over the people God has placed around you. Tomorrow is never promised, but His faithfulness always is.

We hope you enjoy them all!_

## Two Meetings with President Bush
I was in Iraq in support of the first rotation into the country following the invasion, which I had also taken part in. My unit was scheduled to be at the Baghdad Airport facility for the Thanksgiving holiday in 2003. Upon arrival, the first thing we noticed was the presence of Secret Service personnel—something I had never seen in a war zone before. When I went around the corner to the food line, there was President George W. Bush serving slices of turkey. When I got in front of him, he looked up and said, _“Chaplain, you must have one of the hardest jobs here.”_ I told him it had been tough and thanked him for my turkey. He then said, _“Laura and I will be praying for you, and I would really love to talk to you.”_ Little did I know that on Feb. 4, 2005, as I was lying on my bed at Walter Reed Army Medical Center following my first major injury (81mm mortar blast 5 meters behind me), I was awakened to the shuffle of people coming in, and there to my surprise was President Bush and First Lady Laura Bush. The president pinned my citations for the award of the Bronze Star with Combat “V” (Valor in Combat) and my Purple Heart on my blanket. They prayed for me (no press), then he looked at me and said, _“You look like someone I know.”_ I reminded him about Thanksgiving, and we talked for the next half hour. — _Colonel Collins, Greenwood, South Carolina_
## Turkey the Upside-Down Way
When I was young, my grandpa was in charge of cooking the turkey. The party got going early, and he had a few too many when he put the turkey in the oven. My grandma went to check on the timer to see if it popped and found he had put the turkey in upside down! She thought Thanksgiving would be ruined, but they took the turkey out and carved it anyway. Lo and behold, it was the BEST turkey they had ever eaten! Now we know from cooking shows that the reason it was so good is that the breast side was cooking in all the fat at the bottom of the pan. A happy accident—and all of our family still cooks our turkeys upside down! — _Laura, Sarasota, Florida_
## Whipped Cream Chaos
I had just married into a big family, and we were all gathered around a long table for Thanksgiving. When dessert time came, pumpkin pie was passed around, followed by a can of Reddi-Wip. My husband was sitting across from his older brother, who shook the can, tilted it, and sprayed—but instead of landing on the pie, the whipped cream bounced across the surface, shot over the table, and landed all over my husband. It even left a whipped-cream silhouette of him on the wall behind. He hated that it got brought up every year afterward—but everyone else thought it was hilarious. _—Susan_
## Too Many Cooks, Too Much Gravy
One Thanksgiving, my grandmother, mother, and two aunts were doing all the cooking. My mom asked my dad if he’d help make the gravy outside on the backyard BBQ range. He got it going, and things were going well—until he got distracted being a host. While he was away, my grandfather came by, thought the gravy wasn’t thick enough, and added more flour. Then my uncle stopped by and decided it needed something else, so he tossed in some giblets and more flour. When my dad returned, he noticed the gravy had doubled in size. Without thinking much of it, he just poured it into a bigger pot. That’s when my grandfather passed by again, looked in the new pot, and—convinced there still wasn’t enough—added more water and flour. By the time dinner was served, we had over a gallon of gravy. But it didn’t go to waste! My mom sent everyone home with a jar, and the dogs happily finished the rest. The best part? Everyone actually liked it. Turns out, too many cooks didn’t spoil the gravy after all—it just made it unforgettable. _—C. Boyd, South Carolina_ **_Enjoyed these stories? clean_body: I was in Iraq in support of the first rotation into the country following the invasion, which I had also taken part in. My unit was scheduled to be at the Baghdad Airport facility for the Thanksgiving holiday in 2003. Upon arrival, the first thing we noticed was the presence of Secret Service personnel—something I had never seen in a war zone before. | | | | | | Thursday, November 27, 2025 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1895, signed his third and final will, which sparked controversy because he left a significant portion of his fortune to establish the Nobel Prize. Happy Thanksgiving to all our readers! Our staff will be taking holiday time off to spend with their families, and as a result, our editions this weekend will be a little shorter than usual. Also, please keep an eye out for tomorrow's edition—it will be our first-ever Flyover Christmas Gift Guide, filled with dozens of great gift ideas for all members of your family! If you’ve been wanting an easy way to upgrade your grooming routine, today's sponsor, , is one of the few men’s skincare products we’ve actually seen deliver real results—and it’s now 30% off for Flyover readers. In honor of the holiday, we offer this special opening lineup of Thanksgiving memories from our readers, beginning with a military chaplain's story about President Bush that's never been published before. We hope you enjoy them all! | Two Meetings with President Bush I was in Iraq in support of the first rotation into the country following the invasion, which I had also taken part in. My unit was scheduled to be at the Baghdad Airport facility for the Thanksgiving holiday in 2003. Upon arrival, the first thing we noticed was the presence of Secret Service personnel—something I had never seen in a war zone before. When I went around the corner to the food line, there was President George W. Bush serving slices of turkey. When I got in front of him, he looked up and said, “Chaplain, you must have one of the hardest jobs here.” I told him it had been tough and thanked him for my turkey. He then said, “Laura and I will be praying for you, and I would really love to talk to you.” Little did I know that on Feb. 4, 2005, as I was lying on my bed at Walter Reed Army Medical Center following my first major injury (81mm mortar blast 5 meters behind me), I was awakened to the shuffle of people coming in, and there to my surprise was President Bush and First Lady Laura Bush. The president pinned my citations for the award of the Bronze Star with Combat “V” (Valor in Combat) and my Purple Heart on my blanket. They prayed for me (no press), then he looked at me and said, “You look like someone I know.” I reminded him about Thanksgiving, and we talked for the next half hour. — Colonel Collins, Greenwood, South Carolina Turkey the Upside-Down Way When I was young, my grandpa was in charge of cooking the turkey. The party got going early, and he had a few too many when he put the turkey in the oven. My grandma went to check on the timer to see if it popped and found he had put the turkey in upside down! She thought Thanksgiving would be ruined, but they took the turkey out and carved it anyway. Lo and behold, it was the BEST turkey they had ever eaten! Now we know from cooking shows that the reason it was so good is that the breast side was cooking in all the fat at the bottom of the pan. A happy accident—and all of our family still cooks our turkeys upside down! — Laura, Sarasota, Florida Whipped Cream Chaos I had just married into a big family, and we were all gathered around a long table for Thanksgiving. When dessert time came, pumpkin pie was passed around, followed by a can of Reddi-Wip. My husband was sitting across from his older brother, who shook the can, tilted it, and sprayed—but instead of landing on the pie, the whipped cream bounced across the surface, shot over the table, and landed all over my husband. It even left a whipped-cream silhouette of him on the wall behind. He hated that it got brought up every year afterward—but everyone else thought it was hilarious. —Susan Too Many Cooks, Too Much Gravy One Thanksgiving, my grandmother, mother, and two aunts were doing all the cooking. My mom asked my dad if he’d help make the gravy outside on the backyard BBQ range. He got it going, and things were going well—until he got distracted being a host. While he was away, my grandfather came by, thought the gravy wasn’t thick enough, and added more flour. Then my uncle stopped by and decided it needed something else, so he tossed in some giblets and more flour. When my dad returned, he noticed the gravy had doubled in size. Without thinking much of it, he just poured it into a bigger pot. That’s when my grandfather passed by again, looked in the new pot, and—convinced there still wasn’t enough—added more water and flour. By the time dinner was served, we had over a gallon of gravy. But it didn’t go to waste! My mom sent everyone home with a jar, and the dogs happily finished the rest. The best part? Everyone actually liked it. Turns out, too many cooks didn’t spoil the gravy after all—it just made it unforgettable. —C. Boyd, South Carolina Enjoyed these stories? We’ve got a few more waiting for you on our website.. | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

How did Ukraine manage this without F-16s? | | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | A Su-57 downed... Alessandro led the San Antonio Symphony Orchestra from 1951 to 1976, earning recognition for his performances of Tchaikovsky, Richard Strauss, Brahms, and Beethoven. Happy Thanksgiving! ## Macy's Parade Features Texas Band
L.D. Bell Blue Raider Band from Hurst is one of 11 high school bands—and the [only one from Texas](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkMGu2yAQRb8G7yYCzDPOgkVayYtKr-quS2tsxgYVQwQ0Tv6-Iv2Etxtpro7uPb8_50_0wcf9Jx5k6nNLc6SzBKqV8lzpiWXBaH08cH2VO2a0VB3OkssPEAKkni368JpVZ83C7VV0ZIQelFTqynnnDF63TdGwLoNG0WuxrKPYrOppXPSVPnTnTYMJIbWQXPTjRcqht2oUalyIVsGZ4mR9TflSHb0rbeGVHpQvazq6YFyt98L6G5MTk9N5npejxPZjcqIIfwuTUxvF5PS-EWLK1cEbBc7vDsrqUgpwYF6djzu0zXD6EOBOeUv5AB-hKYAC1WH8U3b_aEGLL_hvpYEz3G7i14_vw7cuG3tvAF8GzRTfD_Th3bd-zfLDyH8BAAD__8Wfk0E)—marching in the Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade. The band's 265 students, along with dozens of staff and chaperones, departed for New York [on six flights](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2ypSAQhVeDGRbdcv0JCCYxe5NOaLWCQg2ChV33Pnc_pbOEl3V1cM75vj9f0xeFGNL2m3Zn-HvNU3KfMzpmVyZ233TOlGxIOy3XeVAh69jThApfEkBiN1kK8Zp0Zc2s7ACVM9C1GrUelKq8sb3SYBFW1K0DUjCooWnB2pVe86vXVTB3GAB2gAqavkZsG6t70P3s3AJKaOVs4Fxq9u6ZtMYrv12pl7xX0Xjm4xTNL4GjwHHNhT-5sC_uyIXrXDaB410hcAS470bgyFQKJZY3ncwpXvJJlj5sXp6LzznKncriQ9pkSPLhl-wp_T238L6_li7534jAsSrGHp8QYzjbTmi17RTiM5B_pvVt8F8AAAD___GrjQk) early Monday morning. Two dads transported instruments, uniforms, and everything else that wouldn't fit on a plane in 18-foot box trucks. "_It's so exciting. I just feel so privileged to get to go with all my friends and family to New York for a week and perform_ ," drum major Caidyn Cumberledge said. During the parade, the band [will perform](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGOpDAQRU9jMiO7cGMIHGxC1ptuiMq4AGsLaBlP09x-BHOECUslfb33_j37J0aO6_QXF3L5M279SsfOlDOlPtMHd49riOuCw7m_MGGgPGMPCh5Sawm2Dxj57E0RnFeh1QU5bWsDxrRKFbOzXnn_gKHC1kAIatAj2LFFa0xdNwRFdNeY1mA1KF01JUBdBdNo03iiQSthFIWYt1TmmW6kkc_tTakctqVgN-f82kX1R0AnoDuOo1z9EMbjegvoLh8BHW8D8nVuKc_ynpEsg_TELD1_kUwYAyV5-cpbV-YZ1__7FN9xnWTAU_4EENBVrakaWwvoiuTC64jMca-tMGpaMPJNln_X8-3gOwAA__-xqYpJ) _Deep in the Heart of Texas_ and Dua Lipa's _Houdini_. The school district, families, and community sponsors are splitting costs for the trip. While they're in New York, the band will visit the Rockefeller Center and the 9/11 Memorial & Museum. They’ll also see a Broadway show and the Radio City Rockettes.

**_Thanks to all of you who submitted your Thanksgiving memories. These are a few of our favorites._** **➤ Doug Lunsford (San Angelo):** My family moved to Midland in 1961 when I was ten years old. We invited family, friends, and neighbors to a big Thanksgiving gathering at our new home, where we set up a table with sawhorses and plywood. The best part was the live chickens my grandfather brought. After he chopped their heads off, they ran around the yard for a bit before he cooked them in a huge cast-iron pot. **➤ Cindy Thompson (Tomball):** My husband and I spent one Thanksgiving setting forms for concrete pads for our new mobile home. Our dinner was hot dogs, beanie weenies, and cold beer. It is one of my best memories. **➤ Ann Gromer:** One Thanksgiving, during a snack break from playing cards, I was looking for something salty and found some jerky on a side table. I opened the bag and helped myself. All of a sudden, my parents' 4 dogs were at my feet. "What are you eating?" my mom asks. "Jerky," I replied. She exclaimed, "Those are the dog treats!" I have gotten dog treats in my Christmas stocking every year since! **➤ Susan Bowman:** Shortly after my husband and I married, we were unable to make a trip home for Thanksgiving. I decided to fix a traditional dinner just for the two of us. I wanted to make cornbread dressing like my mother did, so I called her to get her recipe. She said, "Well, you fix your cornbread and crumble it into your pan. You add turkey drippings until it looks right, and then you add chopped-up onions and celery and your spices until it smells right and..." I decided to go with Stove Top Stuffing instead. I still can't fix a decent dressing, and I still use Stove Top Stuffing. **➤ Teri Hogan:** I was an Air Force brat growing up, and we got to San Antonio in 1961. Every year, we went to the mess hall at Lackland AFB for Thanksgiving dinner. It was a multi-family affair, and I still treasure those delicious meals! **➤ Annette Reed (Cedar Hill):** In 1987, my husband and I visited my parents in Hawkins for Thanksgiving. While we were watching the Cowboys game, I asked, "What is that white stuff on the football field?" We had returned from a three-year military assignment in the Philippines, and I had apparently forgotten all about winter weather. My husband said, "That's snow, and it is really coming down. We need to go home _now_." By the time we got to Terrell, I-20 was a slushy mess. When we finally made it home to Cedar Hill, the game was over. **➤ Some of** our readers' favorite Thanksgiving side dishes are egg noodles (George Henry), jalapeño sausage dressing (Larry Leahy), green chili grits (Lorrain Ruiz), and Cajun squash and shrimp casserole (Mona Hudgens). **➤ Sarah Barganier** said she has prepared the turkey for her Thanksgiving meal for 56 years, earning the title "turkey lady." _Flying together with our sponsor_ **➤ I’m always looking for easy ways to get more protein** in the morning without loading up on sugar—[Javvy Protein Coffee checks both boxes](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLmu2zAQRb-G6mRwhptUsEjwoO7FTYCkE4biyCJCLZAYL38f2E6RPu0czMW598dn_0kpp-XyjWb25T6u_cK3I3MpvPeF73QEWmJaZhoex0Y7RS4T9SjR1AA1uj5Syo9eV9EHGVuo2IOzGrVupawmb7gFtkBgDVAzggJjAYmkc86oSFXyzzAAdIASVHNCtCrqBnQTmAeQQkuOqaz7qUz8UhrzY73yfhrWucp-KmU7hPoisBPYZc485F9PJrCTLcuxwVAHkFBrNqpueYSaQ6PQDBYhKKG6geaN0mXpUxTqQ7iv_x7ch0D7rD6_2dlAfUYj_4Lf4f31_Wd3rnYft1vKOR3WCS0vM6X80iz_N-7V458AAAD__6e-hz0). With 10g of protein and 0g of sugar, it’s become a daily staple for me. I mix one scoop into 8 oz of milk and genuinely enjoy the taste. No jitters, no crash—just energy and a satisfying start to my day. clean_body: L.D. Bell Blue Raider Band from Hurst is one of 11 high school bands—and the —marching in the Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade. | | | | | | Thursday, November 27, 2025 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1915, orchestral conductor was born in Waco. Alessandro led the San Antonio Symphony Orchestra from 1951 to 1976, earning recognition for his performances of Tchaikovsky, Richard Strauss, Brahms, and Beethoven. Happy Thanksgiving! We shared a few Thanksgiving memories from Texas Flyover readers in our Around Texas section below. We hope you enjoy reading them as much as we have. This weekend's editions will be a bit shorter than usual while we spend time with our families. Please keep an eye out for tomorrow's edition—our first-ever Flyover Christmas Gift Guide , filled with dozens of great gift ideas for all the members of your family! Amazon's Black Friday Sale is here. Make sure you are taking advantage of all your Prime Account has to offer with today's sponsor, . Take** The Flyoverwith you. Busy day? Listen to our daily 15-minute podcast while commuting, cooking, or walking the dog—your Flyover fix, anywhere. | | Macy's Parade Features Texas Band L.D. Bell Blue Raider Band from Hurst is one of 11 high school bands—and the —marching in the Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade. The band's 265 students, along with dozens of staff and chaperones, departed for New York early Monday morning. Two dads transported instruments, uniforms, and everything else that wouldn't fit on a plane in 18-foot box trucks. "It's so exciting. I just feel so privileged to get to go with all my friends and family to New York for a week and perform ," drum major Caidyn Cumberledge said. During the parade, the band Deep in the Heart of Texas and Dua Lipa's Houdini. The school district, families, and community sponsors are splitting costs for the trip. While they're in New York, the band will visit the Rockefeller Center and the 9/11 Memorial & Museum. They’ll also see a Broadway show and the Radio City Rockettes. | Flying together with our sponsor Now is the perfect time to sign up for. Get ahead of the holiday rush during the season's biggest sale with curated Amazon picks from our deal experts delivered straight to your inbox. Plus, make sure you know all the perks of your Amazon Prime membership so you can . Our team is already hard at work tracking the best early deals to give you a head start. Don't miss out on the chance to on everything on your list. | Thanks to all of you who submitted your Thanksgiving memories. These are a few of our favorites. ➤ Doug Lunsford (San Angelo): My family moved to Midland in 1961 when I was ten years old. We invited family, friends, and neighbors to a big Thanksgiving gathering at our new home, where we set up a table with sawhorses and plywood. The best part was the live chickens my grandfather brought. After he chopped their heads off, they ran around the yard for a bit before he cooked them in a huge cast-iron pot. ➤ Cindy Thompson (Tomball): My husband and I spent one Thanksgiving setting forms for concrete pads for our new mobile home. Our dinner was hot dogs, beanie weenies, and cold beer. It is one of my best memories. ➤ Ann Gromer: One Thanksgiving, during a snack break from playing cards, I was looking for something salty and found some jerky on a side table. I opened the bag and helped myself. All of a sudden, my parents' 4 dogs were at my feet. "What are you eating?" my mom asks. "Jerky," I replied. She exclaimed, "Those are the dog treats!" I have gotten dog treats in my Christmas stocking every year since! ➤ Susan Bowman: Shortly after my husband and I married, we were unable to make a trip home for Thanksgiving. I decided to fix a traditional dinner just for the two of us. I wanted to make cornbread dressing like my mother did, so I called her to get her recipe. She said, "Well, you fix your cornbread and crumble it into your pan. You add turkey drippings until it looks right, and then you add chopped-up onions and celery and your spices until it smells right and..." I decided to go with Stove Top Stuffing instead. I still can't fix a decent dressing, and I still use Stove Top Stuffing. ➤ Teri Hogan: I was an Air Force brat growing up, and we got to San Antonio in 1961. Every year, we went to the mess hall at Lackland AFB for Thanksgiving dinner. It was a multi-family affair, and I still treasure those delicious meals! ➤ Annette Reed (Cedar Hill): In 1987, my husband and I visited my parents in Hawkins for Thanksgiving. While we were watching the Cowboys game, I asked, "What is that white stuff on the football field?" We had returned from a three-year military assignment in the Philippines, and I had apparently forgotten all about winter weather. My husband said, "That's snow, and it is really coming down. We need to go home now." By the time we got to Terrell, I-20 was a slushy mess. When we finally made it home to Cedar Hill, the game was over. ➤ Some of our readers' favorite Thanksgiving side dishes are egg noodles (George Henry), jalapeño sausage dressing (Larry Leahy), green chili grits (Lorrain Ruiz), and Cajun squash and shrimp casserole (Mona Hudgens). ➤ Sarah Barganier said she has prepared the turkey for her Thanksgiving meal for 56 years, earning the title "turkey lady." Flying together with our sponsor ➤ I’m always looking for easy ways to get more protein in the morning without loading up on sugar—. With 10g of protein and 0g of sugar, it’s become a daily staple for me. I mix one scoop into 8 oz of milk and genuinely enjoy the taste. No jitters, no crash—just energy and a satisfying start to my day. name: Team Jack for New York
subject: All in for Schlossberg!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blue Amp Action
body:
The leader NY-12 deserves
| | | | |
---
| | | | |
---
| _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T97c05d1e-8a49-4788-b673-ce782cb18bca/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
| Peter, in case you missed it, Jack is running for Congress in NY-12 — the district where Jack and five generations of his family have called home. We have to ask: Can you spot the difference between these two Kennedys? We can think of a couple... [](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tef94a022-5d89-4511-a783-45d93261a7cf/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) For one, Jack has _NEVER_ killed a bear cub and left it in Central Park! Way to go, Jack 🥳 And if you listen closely, you'll hear thoughtful plans for a stronger, more affordable New York City from the Kennedy on the left and vaccine conspiracy theories from the one on the right. NY-12 deserves a leader who has a plan for standing up to MAGA and MAHA extremism, and Peter, that's Jack to a tee. He won't back down from a tough fight, even if it's with his literal cousin. We're so happy to have you on the team, and we can't wait to get to work for New York and people like YOU. [If you believe in Jack as much as we do, would you do us the honor of splitting a donation between Jack’s campaign and Blue Amp Action? We need your help in this first stretch to show everyone that Jack has the momentum to win! ](https://default.salsalabs.org/Ta76f0af9-a42a-450d-99e2-0feab95cbb55/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade, it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in a democracy, not a dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T9cee9f0b-d6ca-48e7-b735-b930e708c20a/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

name: Ted Cruz
subject: Happy Thanksgiving!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ted Cruz for Senate
body:
Wishing You and Your Family a Very Happy Thanksgiving

| | |
---
| | Dear Fellow Conservative, As we gather with loved ones this Thanksgiving, I hope you find time to reflect on the blessings we enjoy as Americans--family, faith, freedom, and the opportunities that make our Nation strong. In a season that reminds us to pause and give thanks, may your home be filled with warmth, joy, and fellowship. I am especially grateful for the brave men and women serving our country, who sacrifice time with their families so that we may celebrate in safety and peace. This verse speaks deeply to the heart of Thanksgiving: **" Give thanks to the Lord, for He is good; His love endures forever."** **-- Psalm 107:1** Wishing you a blessed and meaningful Thanksgiving, For Liberty, Ted Cruz
---
| | | Paid for by Ted Cruz for Senate
---
| | PO Box 25400
Houston, TX 77265
---
| |

Friend,
---
On 11/21, the Democrat Attorney General in Arizona had the chance to do the right thing and DROP her wrongful prosecution against me – an _innocent_ 80-year-old man facing 20 years in prison.
---
_But at the _final_ _hour__ , she chose bloodthirsty **VENGEANCE** over **JUSTICE** , and decided to continue her prosecution against me and put me on _TRIAL_ right after the holidays are over.
---
**She is now rushing to the __State__ Supreme Court for emergency assistance.**
---
It’s all viciously calculated. She knows the President has no pardon authority for __state__ crimes.
---
But let me be clear: **I will never give up the fight for my freedom**.
---
When President Trump and I were arrested in the most violent jail in America, we proclaimed, “ _NEVER_ SURRENDER!”
---
Two years later, President Trump is back in the White House.
---
_**But I’m still fighting for my freedom!** And Democrats have RIGGED the case so that a federal pardon from President Trump is **_POWERLESS_** in _state_ court._
---
[](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=5f67d781f6cb4c1a835e15b2f0ebfc3c&_e=uoWNIG1V3276TwyuFCF_Yjnbp23-F-i9uEsWnKj8VgdteJ2_Cg1Y9IrnKs-RsouJf0E4npnWo_1QwJvhBI3N4Crh_yjUqMTx8DknSIy6qWGcFPjCu7QLIqIZhm_LeBNnubEXJQIqhs6EgsLsjC-UYj-kWgpJMkgqg5I6UOTFnQWpWXVtTbhns6_jVnzo_ufvwbslDfgjU7oIbD0pLZyIit4m4Jmv9SQlOFvwvNW5IdnEBRCbzEdJPCEeYrEv7S433iXoYXph6-i1ZIPYOyAx-D-MJZTKzzDyOJ2pEl_WgrdA6GDT9D9WgjbK4M-XVaJgWyTDG3VyY7WiRhqi4QJgU5Nh-w1kAguTHXoJQmg4OFLabFwgth9JBv-j3aIhj8JbwyuKclIPoV6GCMGRczutDHDop2NEc5l4fIt1INfL8FxmLlUnBN73Szl5kzl_0k6Nd20ww-at2YlgEBYfzpgcYIK6qOIDYAeZEQWCEPVD9vmyP2i407DNhhpos_bauEqmkXggguy2KRaEbqSad646oX5owCBveweynmhFPR4a-Vkjo7x9r0HFKi3OTabstZUmk9-AGHZPeD1Y8iovrh1zQq3OQB4KiMGJnxvTI9vlYKwyz2wDdvyOCFudrob09nbAQ1fb1CL0P0l6yrJ5hfnBDw%3D%3D)
__
---
The night I was arrested in the most violent jail in America, the Left filed my mugshot under **booking # 2313757.**
---
_Now, to show these left-wing crooks that good and honest Americans like _YOU_ will not stand by as they destroy the life of an INNOCENT man all for _successfully_ defending President Trump as his lawyer, I’m humbly asking for your support to raise **$231,375.70** for my defense trust to retain a top-notch defense team to fight for my _FREEDOM_._
---
Let me put it bluntly: _you_ truly are my final hope.
---

name: Marsha Blackburn
subject: Happy Thanksgiving!
body:
I would like to take a moment to express my sincere gratitude to you.

| | | | Peter,
I wanted to take a moment to wish you a Happy Thanksgiving.
Thanksgiving is a time to be with loved ones and a time to reflect on the many blessings in our lives. This Thanksgiving, I’m so thankful for my family and my country, and I’m especially grateful for supporters like YOU.
As we head into the holiday season, I hope you'll join me in reflecting on the many blessings we enjoy as Americans, who are fortunate to live in the greatest country on earth, where freedom and opportunities are endless.
Today, while we gather with family and friends to give thanks, we must also take a moment to thank the brave men and women serving our country who are unable to be home this holiday season. It’s because of them that we have the blessing of living freely in this great nation.
From my family to yours, I hope that you have a very Happy Thanksgiving!
\- Marsha Blackburn| | |

name: Michigan COV
subject: Happy Thanksgiving! We Are Thankful for You
body:
| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/d37c0bc2b0b1/breaking-news-citizens-only-voting-amendment-submitted-to-board-of-canvassers-8371261?e=ecf60db114)
---
[|
---](https://michcov.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=12ae025e5876fdceb812e1ab0&id=2cf1d248a9&e=ecf60db114)
| | | | | [DONATE TODAY](https://michcov.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=12ae025e5876fdceb812e1ab0&id=71c2c8d823&e=ecf60db114)
---
|
---
| |

Dear Friend,
As we gather with family and friends this Thanksgiving, all of us at Americans for Citizen Voting Michigan want to take a moment to say something simple and heartfelt: thank you.
We are grateful for every volunteer who has stood outside in the cold, spent evenings and weekends gathering signatures, and dedicated hundreds of hours to making sure the voices of Michigan citizens are heard. Your commitment is the backbone of this movement.
We are grateful for every donor who has stepped up to support this effort. Your generosity has powered our ability to educate voters, print materials, support circulators, and keep this effort moving forward. You are investing in Michigan’s future, and it shows.
We are grateful for every elected official, candidate, community leader, neighbor, and supporter who has signed the petition, shared it with others, or encouraged people to get involved. Your willingness to speak up is how we grow, energize, and win.
Because of you, this movement is strong. Because of you, we are nearing a victory that will strengthen the integrity of Michigan elections for generations to come. And because of you, we are proving that when citizens work together, we can achieve real and lasting change.
This Thanksgiving, we want you to know that you are making this happen. You are the reason we are on track to pass this amendment and ensure that only United States citizens vote in Michigan elections. Together, we are protecting our state’s future.
From all of us at Americans for Citizen Voting, we wish you and your family a blessed, warm, peaceful, and joyful Thanksgiving.
With gratitude,
Americans for Citizen Voting Michigan
**P.S.** We are so thankful for everything you’ve done, but we’re not finished yet. Let’s keep our momentum strong, keep the pressure on, and run through the tape together. With your continued energy and commitment, we will finish this fight and deliver a historic win for Michigan.

---
|
---
| | We are going to need our entire grassroots nation to get out and support this effort. Go to our website to sign up for updates, like and follow us on social media and help us spread the word. This issue will be critical to 2026 and we need you!

name: Governor Kelly Ayotte
subject: Happy Thanksgiving!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kelly for New Hampshire, Chris Connelly Treasurer, PO Box 4723 Manchester, NH 03108
body:
Wishing you a day full of family, friends, laughter, and delicious food.

| | | | | |
---
| | | | | | Friend,
Happy Thanksgiving! Wishing you and your loved ones a day full of family, friends, laughter, and delicious food.
Today and every day, I’m thankful for my wingman, Joe, our amazing kids, Kate and Jake, and our dog Thunder. I’m also thankful for a good morning run, my parents, and the opportunity to serve as Governor of the best state in the nation!
Our family is especially grateful to live in the greatest country on earth. We’re thinking of all of our military service members, law enforcement, and first responders who are away from their families this Thanksgiving while they protect our freedom and keep our communities safe. Please join us in thanking them for their service and sacrifice.
I also want to thank you for your support throughout my first year in office. It’s an honor to work on behalf of our wonderful state every day, and we couldn’t do it without you.
Joe, the kids, and I wish you a very Happy Thanksgiving, and we’re looking forward to a great year ahead!
All the best,Kelly
---
| | Paid for by Kelly for New Hampshire, Chris Connelly Treasurer, PO Box 4723 Manchester, NH 03108
---

Hello and Happy Thanksgiving to you and yours! I hope that you are all set to have a wonderful holiday.
My mom went to heroic lengths to host Thanksgiving for us every year growing up. Perhaps because we were immigrants, she took extra steps to commemorate American traditions for my brother and me. She painstakingly spent all day preparing a turkey for us and extended family.
Secret: my brother disliked turkey. He found it to be too dry. But he never wanted to admit it because my Mom worked so hard on the meal so he would drench it in gravy or cranberry sauce. We would have leftovers for days, lurking in the fridge.
Now, as a father, I want to pass a sense of Thanksgiving tradition on to my family. But I am a total non-factor in the kitchen and Evelyn is not really the type to spend all day preparing a meal for 10+ people. There were a couple years when we went to a local restaurant and bought the "Thanksgiving Package" - turkey, stuffing, gravy, mashed potatoes, Brussels sprouts, cranberry sauce, the whole nine. We even had a few relatives over to join us. Everyone seemed to appreciate it, but we've been bringing the kids on the road for the holidays more and more as they've grown.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/0b8ab0d8-7bf3-4a8e-b5b7-0517848c57c1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
So what are we doing this year? Visiting Evelyn's parents in Florida and going to a place where they have a giant Thanksgiving meal! That has become something of a new tradition for us: visiting grandma and grandpa, warm weather, seeing some friends in the area. The sunshine diverges from my childhood memories of this holiday, but as long as the kids associate family with Thanksgiving, I feel like we will have done the job.
I hope that you are able to spend the holiday weekend with family and friends. Gratitude and companionship are two of the biggest indicators of happiness. I have a file with a list of things to be grateful for that I refer to anytime I need a lift. Thanksgiving is a fine time for you to make a list of your own - we all have much to be thankful for.
I'm grateful to you for reading this newsletter and keeping up with me! See how it works? Have a wonderful holiday!
Email [matt@noblemobile.com](https://substack.com/redirect/901ae140-84a2-42d2-9921-d4af0df82eb4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) if you want a few months off from your wireless bill and want to be rewarded with [Noble Mobile](https://substack.com/redirect/867081cf-0689-4840-8788-7f502a2c2f72?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). [Offline](https://substack.com/redirect/810b0220-008c-47d8-af9d-f2f1788b9602?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)'s IRL no-phones party is coming to Boston, Baltimore, and back to NYC in December. My new [book](https://substack.com/redirect/a71b27d3-4b6e-449c-b924-1e8007553735?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) comes out in February. Remember to look up and touch grass.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/7afe9d75-a1bd-42ff-bc51-dda792490ca7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

name: Tim Burchett
subject: Grateful. Thankful. Blessed.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Burchett for Congress**
body:
A Thanksgiving note from Tim 🦃

| | | |
---
| |
---
| |
---
friend, As you gather around the table today, I hope you’re surrounded by family, laughter, and a sense of hope. I know the news cycle never seems to slow down, and if it feels like we’re constantly going at it up in Washington—you’re probably right. But even in the chaos, we have so much to be grateful for. President Trump has made real strides for the American people over the past year, and I’m proud to work alongside him and my fellow Republicans to keep pushing the America First agenda forward. We live in the most free and prosperous country in the dadgum world. Don’t let anyone on the Left tell you anything different. We owe that blessing to our Lord and Savior, Jesus Christ—and to the brave men and women who defend this nation. They’re the reason we can take today off and enjoy a home-cooked meal with the people we love. Happy Thanksgiving, . Serving you and fighting for the conservative values we share is the honor of my lifetime. Thank you for sending me here! Sincerely,
---
---
Tim Burchett
---
| | |
---
**Stay in Touch 👇** **Paid for by Burchett for Congress**

name: Josh Hawley
subject: Happy Thanksgiving
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Josh Hawley Victory Committee
body:
| | |
---
| |
---
We live in strange times, and many of the loudest voices paint a picture of doom and gloom. But we have so much to be thankful for as a nation, in my home state of Missouri, and in my own family. So however you are celebrating this important holiday, or whoever you are celebrating with, take a moment to reflect on all there is to be grateful for. Here is one of my favorite verses; I hope it is an encouragement to you: _Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good; his love endures forever. _—Psalm 118:29
---
---
| | God Bless,
---
---
Josh Hawley
---

clean_body: | | | | | We live in strange times, and many of the loudest voices paint a picture of doom and gloom. But we have so much to be thankful for as a nation, in my home state of Missouri, and in my own family. So however you are celebrating this important holiday, or whoever you are celebrating with, take a moment to reflect on all there is to be grateful for. Here is one of my favorite verses; I hope it is an encouragement to you: Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good; his love endures forever. —Psalm 118:29 | | God Bless, Josh Hawley | | | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- | | | Paid for by Josh Hawley Victory Committee | | |

name: Kelvin King
subject: From our family to yours...
body:
Happy Thanksgiving | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/lwfaa/icymi-kelvin-king-for-ga-sec-of-state-14195519?e=cb396c52a2)
---
| |
---
| |
# _Dear Friends and Supporters,_
This Thanksgiving, Janelle and I want to take a moment to wish you and your loved ones a wonderful celebration filled with family, friends, and gratitude.

During this time, let’s remember to focus on what truly matters—our connections with one another—and not get caught up in the political drama that can often surround us. I am sincerely thankful for the opportunity to run for office once again and to serve the great people of Georgia as your next Secretary of State. We are seeing great momentum on the campaign trail! We've been busy raising funds and gathering support. Recently, I spent a few days in South Georgia, meeting with dedicated rural nurses and business owners. Their passion and commitment to their communities inspire me to work even harder.
Enjoy this festive season, and I look forward to continuing our journey together. _Happy Thanksgiving, from our family to yours!_
---

body:

---
| | | | Hey Peter,

In Tennessee’s 7th District, a seat Republican for decades, something shocking is happening. A verified report shows **turnout for the GOP is plummeting while Democrats are surging.**

This is exactly the kind of moment we’ve been preparing for.
* When the opposition’s turnout collapses, it opens the door for winning overlooked districts.
* [If we act now, we can invest in the groundwork and outreach that turns this chance into a win.](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KB2S5V663K11E1A0T6A8FCGA_1)

If we don’t meet this moment, the GOP will regroup and the opening will shut. [**Donate Now**](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KB2S5V663K11E1A0T6A8FCGA_2)
– Jesse, DemsGive

| Friend As families across America gather around the table today, all of us at Team Imer PAC want to take a moment to say thank you.
Thank you for standing up for our values when it's not always easy. Thank you for believing that our nation is worth fighting for. We thank you for being part of an America First movement that refuses to surrender our freedoms, our families, or our future.
This year, we're especially grateful for grassroots patriots like you who knock doors, share the truth on social media, talk to friends and neighbors, and support bold conservative leaders.
Together, we're building a stronger Republican Party from the ground up--one that defends President Trump's legacy, protects our communities, and puts America First every single time.
As we look ahead to the crucial battles coming up, we're committed to recruiting, training, and electing unapologetic conservative voices--and we can't do it without you. Your prayers, your time, and your support fuel everything we do.
From our Team Imer PAC family to yours, we wish you a blessed, safe, and Happy Thanksgiving. May God continue to bless you, your family, and the United States of America.
---
|
---
| With gratitude,

name: Jocelyn Benson
subject: From my family to yours, happy Thanksgiving!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOCELYN BENSON FOR GOVERNOR
body:
# [](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/203?t=1&akid=1035%2E181023%2E6jMkLg)
Hi there, happy Thanksgiving!
Whether you’re running a turkey trot or watching the Thanksgiving parade cuddled up with loved ones, I hope your Thanksgiving is joyful and restful.
My husband Ryan, our son Aiden, and I are excited to eat good food, watch the Lions beat the Packers, and reflect on what we’re thankful for — and that includes you.
Running a campaign for Governor here in Michigan is no easy feat, but every day I am grateful for the people I’ve met who have shared their stories, pitched in, and rallied behind our vision.
I am filled with extra gratitude this holiday season for the people who make Michigan home. Over the coming year, I look forward to continuing to share my vision for a better, more affordable future for us all to make Michigan the best place to be a kid, raise a kid, and call home.
Thank you and happy Thanksgiving,
Jocelyn

* * *
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with supporters like you.
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe-jbmi/?t=2&akid=1035%2E181023%2E6jMkLg)

PAID FOR BY JOCELYN BENSON FOR GOVERNOR

23133 Woodward Ave, Number 116,
Ferndale, MI 48220

name: Tracey Mann
subject: Wishing You and Your Family a Happy Thanksgiving
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mann for Congress, Inc
body:
From my family to yours, Happy Thanksgiving. Thank you for the opportunity to serve you in Congress. | | | [ ](https://traceymann.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60a16ea89a176679a5f31366c&id=9f79446e76&e=a9d1410029)
---
| |
---
| **Dear Kansan,**

As we gather with family and friends this Thanksgiving, I want to take a moment to say THANK YOU for the privilege of serving you and our great Big First district in Congress. _**Representing Kansas is an honor I don’t take lightly, and I’m grateful every day for the trust you’ve placed in me.**_

Thanksgiving is a time to pause and reflect on the many blessings we enjoy — faith, family, freedom, and the strong communities that make Kansas such a special place to call home. I’m especially grateful for:
* The **farmers and ranchers** whose hard work feeds and fuels the world.
* The **small business owners** and workers who keep our Main Streets vibrant.
* The **teachers, healthcare workers, first responders, and law enforcement officers** who serve others every day.
* The **men and women in uniform** , and their families, who sacrifice so much to defend our country.

As you celebrate this week — whether around a crowded table or in a quieter season of life — I hope you find time for rest, gratitude, and connection with the people who matter most.

>From my family to yours, Happy Thanksgiving. It is an honor to work for you in Congress.

**Yours in the fight,**

Tracey Mann

name: Seth Moulton
subject: Gratitude in motion
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP25/1/74758/images/Email%20Wrapper%20Header%203.png]

Hey friend,

Before my team and I head out to run the Feaster Five Road Race in North Andover this morning, I just wanted to take a moment to say thank you.

Thanksgiving is a reminder to slow down, take stock, and appreciate the people who make our lives and our communities stronger. For me, that’s the veterans, teachers, parents, small business owners, and neighbors I’ve met all across Massachusetts who keep showing up, even when it’s hard.

They’re the reason I got into public service, and they’re what keep me optimistic about where we’re headed because no matter how tough things get, people here still show up for each other.

I hope you get a chance today to rest, recharge, and spend time with the people you love.

Happy Thanksgiving,

Seth
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP25/1/74758/images/moulton-header-headshot-3.jpg]

About Seth Moulton
Seth Moulton is a father, a husband, a Marine, and a Democrat with a mission to bring a new generation of leaders to Congress. He is the proud father of two girls, Emmy and Caroline, and husband to his wife, Liz. They live in Salem, Massachusetts.
Follow Seth

| | _From our family to yours,_**_Happy Thanksgiving!_** _During this season of gratitude, we give thanks for the many blessings in our lives: our family, friends, neighbors, and the grace of God._ _We also pause to honor and pray for the brave men and women in our military, law enforcement, and first responders whose daily sacrifices keep us safe and free. _ _We hope your Thanksgiving is filled with warmth, joy, and good food shared around the table._ _With gratitude, _**_Addison & Rachel McDowell_**

# 💙If You've Ever Thought About Joining Us--Today's the Day. 75% Off Just For You.
### Join BAM for $19.99 and help us take the fight straight to the fascists.
| | | [Cliff Schecter](https://substack.com/@cliffsedge) and [Blue Amp](https://substack.com/@blueamp)
---
| Nov 27
---
| [](https://substack.com/@cliffsedge)| | [](https://substack.com/@blueamp)
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/277fdaa0-9ac4-4009-bff1-e8b4cae2e8b5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Hey friends,
It's Cliff. And I'm just gonna speak from the heart.
We're heading into 2026, a year that decides if our country still belongs to all of us--or those who'd burn it down for more power, or an extra billion. While new "pro-democracy" voices pop up every week, _we 've been here fighting this fight all along._
We are Never-Trump. But we were also Never-Bush and Never-McConnell.
**Blue Amp Media** (**BAM**) is built on that integrity and fire.
On independent voices.
On refusing to bend.
[75% Off - Support BAM for $0.38 per Week](https://substack.com/redirect/277fdaa0-9ac4-4009-bff1-e8b4cae2e8b5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
But for us to rise to this moment, **we need you with us**. We've seen those who were Republican or corporate media just a few years--or months--ago get the support _**we need** to survive and grow_.
We'd like to extend our hand and show you how critical it is that we have you behind us:
#### 🔥 [75% OFF BAM Annual Membership -- $19.99](https://substack.com/redirect/277fdaa0-9ac4-4009-bff1-e8b4cae2e8b5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) 🔥
Not just an incredible deal.
A commitment from us.
And a promise from you, you're stepping into the fight with us.
If you've ever thought, "I should join them someday," let that someday be today. Again, _this is that moment_ when we either sink or swim. And either way, your choice will determine our path.
[👆 Join us for $19.99](https://substack.com/redirect/277fdaa0-9ac4-4009-bff1-e8b4cae2e8b5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Help us carry the truth into the most important election of our lives. This isn't a fight we want to walk away from.
And we know you don't, either.
--Cliff
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/277fdaa0-9ac4-4009-bff1-e8b4cae2e8b5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

subject: 🇺🇸 Kevin Kijewski for AG - Happy Thanksgiving Michigan! 🇺🇸
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kevin Kijewski for Michigan, 115 W Allegan St, Ste 900, Lansing, MI 48933_
body:
| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/kevinformi/kevin-kijewski-for-ag-happy-thanksgiving-michigan-4741049?e=4b10910eed)

[|

| | | | | |
# Happy Thanksgiving Michigan!
---
| | Dear Friend and Patriot,
[Happy Thanksgiving](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=606ef78e55&e=4b10910eed)! I'm truly grateful for the blessings of family, freedom, and this incredible state we all love. As we come together with our loved ones today cheering on the Lions, let's hold onto the values that keep America strong: faith in God, hard work, and standing united. Nothing tops kicking off the day with the people who matter most.
I am especially thankful you for your continued support of our campaign to become Michigan’s next Attorney General. [Together, we’ll flip Michigan red, restore public safety, defend constitutional rights, and ensure election integrity for all](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=29290f7c46&e=4b10910eed). God bless you and your family! 🇺🇸
---
| | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=8f5f433338&e=4b10910eed)
| | I’m looking forward to meeting with additional Republican delegates and groups in December from across the state. The momentum of our movement to reclaim the state from the radical left continues to build, and November 2026 can't arrive soon enough.
As Michigan's next Attorney General, I’ll fight to uphold the rule of law, prosecute dangerous criminals, protect our Second Amendment rights, stop political prosecutions, and defend the hardworking people of this state from government overreach. [To win against the radical left's agenda, please contribute today and join the movement to take Michigan back!](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=315f40f6a6&e=4b10910eed)
Very truly yours,Kevin Kijewski
Candidate for Michigan Attorney General
---

---](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=8cc029ebe1&e=4b10910eed)

name: Chris Pappas
subject: Happy Thanksgiving
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Pappas for Senate
body:
Wishing you a joyful holiday season and sharing my deepest thanks.
#
Friend,
As we gather with loved ones this Thanksgiving, I want to take a moment to say thank you.
The campaign we’re building together is powered by people like you who believe in something better: a government that works for everyone, a democracy that delivers on its promises, and a future where every family has a fair shot.
This time of year is about reflection, gratitude, and connection. For me that means spending time with family and cooking delicious meals. These simple traditions remind me why I do this work and who I’m fighting for every day.
So from the bottom of my heart, thank you for being part of this campaign. Whether you’ve chipped in, volunteered, shared a message, or simply believed in what we’re doing, your support means the world to me.
Wishing you and your family a joyful, peaceful, and safe Thanksgiving from here in Manchester.
Warmly,
Chris
**[Donate »](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251127_1_thanksgiving.pMY9lm&refcode=em20251127-1&t=1&akid=3705%2E582294%2E0zPJRo)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.chrispappas.org/signup/cps_receive_fewer_emails/?t=2&akid=3705%2E582294%2E0zPJRo)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.chrispappas.org/signup/cps_update_your_information/?t=3&akid=3705%2E582294%2E0zPJRo)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.chrispappas.org/unsubscribe/cps_unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=3705%2E582294%2E0zPJRo)

Email us: [teampappas@chrispappas.org](mailto:teampappas@chrispappas.org)
Paid for by Chris Pappas for Senate
Chris Pappas for Senate
PO Box 313
Manchester, NH 03105

name: Citizens Alliance New Hampshire
subject: 🦃 Thankful for Liberty, Grateful for You
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CITIZENS ALLIANCE OF NEW HAMPSHIRE]()
body:
Thank you for standing with us, for believing in a freer New Hampshire, and for helping us build a legacy of liberty...

| | | | | [|
---](https://citizensalliancenh.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9ec7dd6f72fe3fc1550cd2695&id=2e4fb5f7d6&e=8c8e704ee6)
| | | | | | Peter,

**As we gather with loved ones this Thanksgiving, we at Citizens Alliance of New Hampshire have so much to be thankful for — starting with YOU!**

Thanks to your support, _**98 liberty-loving lawmakers**_ have signed The Pledge and are committed to upholding the values that make New Hampshire great.

Each of the 98 Pledge signers is committed to sponsoring and upholding legislation that defends the principles of the New Hampshire and U.S. Constitutions.

**Thanks to your dedication, we’re entering next session with a team of champions who will fight for the values that keep New Hampshire a sanctuary of freedom.**
---
| | **This Thanksgiving, we are grateful for you...**

Whether you volunteered, donated, or simply stood with us in spirit, your commitment to preserving liberty has been the heart of our mission.

We are also thankful for the principled lawmakers who will stand up for our shared values in the legislature next year.

These lawmakers will bring bold ideas to Concord, fighting for the freedoms we hold dear and building a foundation for a state where liberty thrives.

Next year, we’ll continue building on this momentum, rallying more support and electing even more champions to ensure New Hampshire remains the Live Free or Die state for generations to come.

Thank you for standing with us, for believing in a freer New Hampshire, and for helping us build a legacy of liberty.

From our family to yours, we wish you a Happy Thanksgiving.

These lawmakers will bring bold ideas to Concord, fighting for the freedoms we hold dear and building a foundation for a state where liberty thrives. body:

| | Peter,
As we gather with family and friends this Thanksgiving, I want to share a heartfelt message of gratitude.
I’m thankful for Nebraska, for our strong communities, and for people like you who make this state such a remarkable place to call home. Your engagement, your support, and your belief in our shared values mean more than you know.

With gratitude,
Brett Lindstrom
