name: Senator Dick Anderson
subject: Happy Thanksgiving from Senator Anderson
body:
| | | [](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fcontent.govdelivery.com%2Faccounts%2FORLEG%2Fbulletins%2F3fd03e1/1/0101019ac5d6382c-ecf4c1cf-81d8-4604-848c-398c16ebcf37-000000/NVh7rif_mvBh5Hry6EEekLIRSufLM_OPqfQsj0yGrZA=433 "View Online")
---|---
---
|
* * *
---
Dear Friends and Neighbors,
As the crisp coastal winds blow in and the rain taps steadily on our roofs, Thanksgiving arrives once again to remind us of the simple, profound gifts we share here on the Oregon Coast. From the fishing boats in Newport and Coos Bay to the cranberry bogs of South Coast, from the timber families in Reedsport to the retirees enjoying the quiet beauty of Florence and Yachats, this season calls us to pause and give thanks, together.
I’m grateful every single day for the honor of representing you in Salem. Whether it’s fighting for our fishing families, pushing for real wildfire mitigation and forest health, standing up for our rural hospitals, or working across the aisle to keep our communities safe and affordable, your voices guide me. You’ve shared your stories at coffee chats in Philomath, town halls in North Bend, and over pie at the Lincoln County Fair, and I carry them with me.
This year has not been without its challenges. Rising costs, housing pressures, and the lingering impacts of storms and wildfires have tested us. Yet time and again, I’ve seen neighbors checking on neighbors, volunteers filling the need of our community, fire department crews helping our tourists, and families opening their doors to those in need. That’s Oregon Coast strong. That’s who we are.
Thanksgiving reminds me of when I was in Coos Bay this fall, I heard our Native American friends talk about when times get hard, we gather. The tribes gather and solve the problems together. In my mind, Thanksgiving is a great time to gather with your tribe no matter how big or small, they are the ones who will be there to help solve problems.
To our veterans and active-duty service members (especially those far from home this Thanksgiving), thank you. To our first responders, healthcare workers, teachers, and small-business owners who keep our communities running, thank you. And to every family gathering around a table, big or small, thank you for holding tight to the values that make the District 5 such a special place for me and my family to call “home”.
May your Thanksgiving be filled with good food (Dungeness crab counts, right?), laughter, and the warmth of those you love. My wife Sue and I are counting our blessings, and you, our constituents and friends, are at the very top of that list.
From our family to yours, Happy Thanksgiving.
Gratefully,
Dick
Oregon State Senator – District 5
P.S. – My district office is always open to you. If you’re struggling with a state agency, have an idea for legislation, or just want to say hello, don’t hesitate to reach out: 503-986-1705 or Sen.DickAnderson@oregonlegislature.gov.
* * *
---

Enjoyed sharing stories with neighbors at the chamber of commerce dinner in Lincoln City.
* * *
---

Lincoln Cultural Center dedication of Mosaic art piece in the courtyard. I had the opportunity to address the crowd with Mayor Walke of Lincoln City
* * *
---
We attended the 2 day conference - Governor to Government with the tribal groups in Coos Bay last month.
* * *
At the end of September the Coastal Caucus had a great turnout for the Economic Summit in Coos Bay and North Bend. Legislators and interested parties came from all over the state to attend.
* * *

Senior fair was a great time in Lincoln City. Remember you can always contact my office for help with any state agency.
* * *
Sue and I had fun helping collect donations for the Stuff a Bus program.
* * *
Finally, this past "Legislative Days" we were able to host Ambassador Marco Sermoneta, Consul General of Israel to my office in Salem. It was a real pleasure to spend some time with him discussing many topics important to our friends in the jewish community.
* * *
---
* * *
---
In February 2026, the Legislature will convene for 35 days at most to handle some budget fixes and technical fixes to legislation. It is becoming clear these "short sessions" tend to take on big deals. Housing, Measure 110, and many things have been worked on in a very short period of time. I don't see this session changing.
Our office has drafted two bills we will be focused on in Short Session. Housing and Child Care.
Housing will be squarely focused on getting prevailing wage requirements out of affordably housing projects. Prevailing wage is dramatically increasing the cost of affordable housing across Oregon and we need to build more not less affordable housing. We will also be allowing for child care facilities in these projects as well.
Our second bill will be a childcare bill. We will be tackling regulations and liability insurance protections to ensure we get more child care, not less.
I was recently put on Senate Finance and Revenue Committee. This committee deals a lot with the taxes of the state. Rest assured I will be bringing a common sense and less government is better mentality, but we need to have real conversations about taxes across Oregon. I am excited to have these robust conversations with all my colleagues.
* * *
---
* * *
---
**Some news articles I read I found interesting, maybe you will too:**
Why we are fighting the prevailing wage laws in Oregon. [Wweek.com](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.wweek.com%2Fnews%2Fstate%2F2025%2F11%2F18%2Flandmark-eugene-development-stalls-over-a-wage-dispute-with-statewide-impacts%2F/1/0101019ac5d6382c-ecf4c1cf-81d8-4604-848c-398c16ebcf37-000000/FaKewyGJ9AQxgKsuF8TNyrHeadW6w4zN33MSaGSMupg=433)
Audit of BOLI shows there is a problem. [Philomath News ](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fphilomathnews.com%2Faudit-blasts-the-oregon-bureau-of-labor-and-industries%2F/1/0101019ac5d6382c-ecf4c1cf-81d8-4604-848c-398c16ebcf37-000000/ykUG0L7IegV_TaX30NQrlOIAW6xD8K8cGYAIzpQn_Kw=433)
Floating Solar Panels? [Sisuslaw News](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.thesiuslawnews.com%2Fnews%2Ffloating-solar-panels-show-promise-but-environmental-impacts-vary-by-location-study-finds%2Farticle_8eb40791-5ccb-5bd7-9b6b-f59263ee0cf2.html/1/0101019ac5d6382c-ecf4c1cf-81d8-4604-848c-398c16ebcf37-000000/At0QQTk3sbAU51HJKidVx6vCEwuQed4bxd7RC5uy1Jk=433)
Rep. Court Boice takes the helm of the Oregon Coastal Caucus for 2026 session - [The World Newspaper](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Ftheworldlink.com%2Fnews%2Fboice-takes-the-helm-of-oregon-coastal-caucus-for-2026-session%2Farticle_c40b8b34-bcc6-4a90-8cdd-05d299971e5f.html/1/0101019ac5d6382c-ecf4c1cf-81d8-4604-848c-398c16ebcf37-000000/AiVrgb4hCO7A4AU26ZvcHBYxLl_LsUgmKEFOzS1uJN0=433)
What do Native Americans think of Bigfoot?**[OPB Story](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.opb.org%2Farticle%2F2025%2F10%2F23%2Ffrom-legend-to-relative-indigenous-oregonians-share-bigfoots-true-spirit%2F/1/0101019ac5d6382c-ecf4c1cf-81d8-4604-848c-398c16ebcf37-000000/Hr1g5bkiQNYe0832fpLCjlMloDE03TUOEU1WHFT4yos=433)**
* * *
We all have probably read or heard something about the Coast Guard Helicopter removal from Newport to North Bend. Newport is the most central location on the coast for the helicopter in case of emergencies. My office has been working with the federal Department of Homeland Security trying to get it relocated back to Newport for crabbing season.
* * *
---
**Capitol Phone:** 503-986-1705
**Capitol Address:** 900 Court St NE, S-311, Salem, OR, 97301
**Email:**[Sen.DickAnderson@oregonlegislature.gov](mailto:Sen.DickAnderson@oregonlegislature.gov)
**Website:**[http://www.oregonlegislature.gov/anderson](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fanderson/1/0101019ac5d6382c-ecf4c1cf-81d8-4604-848c-398c16ebcf37-000000/w9E2gi24sGZGU4B4PPEDP767rTTwZFcHU6sTASdbeYw=433)
Replies to this message are sent to an unmonitored mailbox.
To contact me, please click here: [Sen.DickAnderson@oregonlegislature.gov](mailto:Sen.DickAnderson@oregonlegislature.gov)
* * *
**SUBSCRIBER SERVICES OPTIONS:**
**•** To **stop** receiving this newsletter, **add** new newsletters, or**delete** your subscription services click [Manage Preferences](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fpublic.govdelivery.com%2Faccounts%2FORLEG%2Fsubscriber%2Fnew%3Fpreferences=true/1/0101019ac5d6382c-ecf4c1cf-81d8-4604-848c-398c16ebcf37-000000/R-Fq9vZ4rl81Q6MjQc9PTSTo3bJTRIr3-Ee-7R_4DLw=433)
**•** For questions about subscriber services, please email [Help](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fsubscriberhelp.govdelivery.com%2F/1/0101019ac5d6382c-ecf4c1cf-81d8-4604-848c-398c16ebcf37-000000/OxruBckkwBUDvsCy-yAFQhZuXsor-UN_TCqZ77jp45w=433) Information provided may not be final and is subject to change.
---
* * *
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com using GovDelivery · 707 17th St, Suite 4000 · Denver, CO 80202 · 1-800-439-1420 | [](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fsubscriberhelp.granicus.com%2F/1/0101019ac5d6382c-ecf4c1cf-81d8-4604-848c-398c16ebcf37-000000/laLwETZXA94ByJ9-GB__MbD1ZgWGIowxdMWoqDIxvxk=433)

name: Social Security Update (VPP)
subject: This is HUGE
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
Democrats’ proposal could change everything.
| [](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/social-poll?source=VPP_20251127_EMPOLL_E1_T0_AC_RS&t=6&akid=22014%2E878179%2EvRB-uQ) | **FIRST** Trump pledged there **“would be cuts”** to Social Security, endangering benefits Americans’ have worked their whole lives for!

**THEN** Rand Paul claimed Republicans **“didn’t have the money”** for Social Security, threatening to RAISE the retirement age to 70!

**_BUT NOW_** Democrats aren’t going down without a fight, they just introduced **a HUGE monthly increase** to Social Security! This changes everything!
---
[**But we’re lacking opinion data in your state. We need to hear from 346 more Democrats:
Should Congress increase Social Security payments?**](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/social-poll?source=VPP_20251127_EMPOLL_E1_T0_AC_RS&t=7&akid=22014%2E878179%2EvRB-uQ)
[YES, ABSOLUTELY! »](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/social-poll?source=VPP_20251127_EMPOLL_E1_T0_AC_RS&t=8&akid=22014%2E878179%2EvRB-uQ)

name: Nancy Pelosi
subject: Happy Thanksgiving!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nancy Pelosi for Congress
body:
https://x.com/TeamPelosi [https://x.com/TeamPelosi]

>>>>>>>>>>>>> [https://x.com/TeamPelosi]

Dear Friends,

As we gather with loved ones this Thanksgiving, may we all reflect on the blessings of faith, family, friends and community that are the heart of our nation.

In challenging times, past and present, the spirit of America endures because we, the people, endure. Strengthened by our shared values, we fight alongside one another for a brighter and better future that is worthy of the vision of our Founders, of the service and sacrifice of our men and women in uniform, and of the dreams and aspirations of our children. Today and every day, I am thankful for your support and unwavering commitment, compassion and courage in this fight.

In this time of hardship for our country, we must continue to be instruments of peace and progress, sowing love where there is hatred and lending a hand to our neighbors in need. Our democracy depends not only on us mobilizing for elections but on the everyday acts of kindness and solidarity that build and strengthen us – that is the true meaning and purpose of community!

As we go forward, together, we must hold onto our faith in America’s goodness and embrace the duty that we owe to the country we love, to one another and to future generations. Thank you from the bottom of my heart for who you are and for all that you do to bring us closer to the more perfect union that we are called upon to create in our Constitution.

From my family to yours, Happy Thanksgiving!
NANCY

STAY IN TOUCH! [https://secure.ngpvan.com/naE7ndwzXE-yTRRAf-V7EA2]

name: Kelly Morrison
subject: Thanksgiving thoughts on kindness and courage
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Morrison for Congress
body:
https://kellyforcongress.org [https://kellyforcongress.org]
Happy Thanksgiving, Peter! 🦃🧡
This year, I find myself deeply grateful for this grassroots team. You have all accomplished so much—from knocking on tens of thousands of doors, to supporting Democratic candidates up and down the ballot, to taking to the streets and marching peacefully to protest the authoritarian creep of this administration - you have made and continue to make such a difference. I am so inspired by this incredible community and feel so grateful to be a part of it.
Peter, this year has been tough and downright frightening, but there are emerging signs of hope. Now more than ever, we need to lean into kindness and courage. We need both if we are to emerge from this dark time intact and ready to build a better future. I’ve been calling for a “kindness revolution” because we need to care for each other and we need to resist this administration’s calls to lean into hatred and division. Resisting that narrative takes courage, and if we are all just a little bit brave, together we will prevail. Kindness and courage will carry us forward and help us meet the challenges ahead with hope and resolve.
I am so proud to be in this effort with all of you, Peter! Thank you for your commitment—it truly means the world. I hope you are able to relax and enjoy some well-deserved time with your loved ones today and this weekend.
Have a very happy Thanksgiving!
With (extra) gratitude,
Kelly Morrison
https://www.facebook.com/KellyMorrisonForCongress [https://www.facebook.com/KellyMorrisonForCongress] https://twitter.com/Morrison4MN [https://twitter.com/Morrison4MN] https://instagram.com/kellymorrisonforcongress [https://instagram.com/kellymorrisonforcongress] kellyforcongress.org [kellyforcongress.org]

Native Americans face major health disparities compared to non-Natives. | | | [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.X6XUC7u8ppFLNtMO_55SZlNseb7gEYIOhm_EWnhzEOZKmTtnV3k2h4V2x_O3H7rdW2az5rgktBwzxQ8Ml9Wgkmv85qW-UsBonL4KRa0bcNqdICU6T3NBDdNIZG7w7wEWY0-7ElSjoYZ5cp96YoKlXZF526Z7bQUI3w9DUAkMYD_W1Yc9iYEArjxhuEy8gpFQzjMIrFQ3FjstFqki_uK32msJqU8WQn-_kHjk6bKj-g6PDx8yJBhI_ve5UZm_PettTwyEWTzbJYDx6pPq8fMTySQITip2O4N_4L_AIBN0nmlnogsP30EQfVutKdEAQqxrv3EFPcDrnwtEgJ0zeUuv140hwOYujmb2McW1_3aMTe4/4ly/gktkkfEER_aqng1Us6NtAw/h0/h001.n4T6qP1Q1YD37W0PFTxF3ZDoBOG5J0QjGQwqsaLSAMc)

Friend, This Thanksgiving, let’s fight colonialism by supporting Indigenous-led organizations. [**Could you chip in to American Indian Health and Family Services, a nonprofit health and wellness center that makes holistic healthcare accessible to Indigenous people in my state of Michigan?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsXu0R1XDWRVJnEMC9_ePsJWbpxxrEDnhV3WcTpCG3Uf41bm2sGs5Mx81SKPHgzVotXxI3mMW5m68IknkI5bVsQHicY-2zORCKbccXrqgnjhwMukokqL7lTwcN-_nV5EcwjWFMyeppPWzsJufWRR2AUkF0Sk_BFa3DL6WOw7U-hRe-cD7n6hj1IFr6sU03au4oR6b8OkDILhLPl7MvRlvfQMt4SonMJ85yyckKXpI9ceO66Erp5LH2YDKHf2A1bbJUSWcoNqA_s-wANcWbs541FZsYIC-jymEBFmR0TgVwCLY0n0HZ4how8jXPN5h2CeT_DOIlPjYPEz48HWmUPZ7msk-oMOK84TqdOPVMd5C_fBdb_Cf8CLl2PfuTdH7JwAns2GOAfutOo9CnofcWqT9w_rcmHoASSknflI4mEXGHpJGuZdrB3tcoWZy-EBjhvOqk/4ly/gktkkfEER_aqng1Us6NtAw/h1/h001.tWyraatKrsL3kq2CZUcJx0gee3R6QBxS1k85a7btsMw) | [SUPPORT NATIVE HEALTH THIS THANKSGIVING](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsXu0R1XDWRVJnEMC9_ePsJWbpxxrEDnhV3WcTpCG3Uf41bm2sGs5Mx81SKPHgzVotXxI3mMW5m68IknkI5bVsQC-Zit73riHPliTF1VcPPP7YTU7YzMe-9ESUXZIQSXHdzrix2srvPajVskmaZGVM30IJiTRrSp14m9vhlryLYucPeTB56T2apqnYCFIPt2pHk8skbbW7ra7Nk0UJcoymVMnEyhvcwfmYGV1qR1mtODyvAbEYFIi5vgF-uAph1OtNa541WZcdozEPDB8_zl2vd-NzhFsws46AvxvoA0hwN_xy1BXfWOJhu7_9ykQRHWMTnb9ZNHZRYIOKF38wLu9rbhaDId8eUn5FGwAW5TTvM1RoLnmUskXzSzxeQFa91khZ1fk5l-BzNEWgfYHIZpQZu5ngy5qyfcIQ9_OC9CY9Z1RXDRQ6K8d3w0RStgIaeqXY/4ly/gktkkfEER_aqng1Us6NtAw/h2/h001.6F-Hfmd6xWhHfxd8vgjwgUfcn2RH7IVFFwUNY0BWPO0)
---
For 47 years **American Indian Health and Family Services has strengthened the health and wellbeing of Michigan’s urban Indigenous community** through high-quality, accessible care that integrates traditional healing and cultural practices. This photo is from one of their annual pow wows:
[](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsXu0R1XDWRVJnEMC9_ePsJWbpxxrEDnhV3WcTpCG3Uf41bm2sGs5Mx81SKPHgzVotXxI3mMW5m68IknkI5bVsQHB2-0cZubY06JYj-EBd3NReUyC7bP70itzeAU0KTCp7rNN2_jV2DADft832biLiB0G_E4fUyJZ77Y7RmdgTkGe5yDSZuRtu-DfPv-OXwY-Viq60iDN2nBZ5zfHBmjMzTWxmpxJnPzdlcgHNUrAGJIVb0XDG8xB6_U-i-_0jkI0rEN-MpLLU2SNZwTU9o08UfbpimZZfcRSNnCl1V9v6MNQ8N-ynpKLeQwu230xL1AxK4apzlGMEFySerqt5mVCuvgJy8iu4xR_xipd1_cB2obhxhmkYacSHW9oxKSGA5G3uie8QAuYJVsCsd49LUuiZMfY_55kQfgI1wIpPa5G_T0VbGtI8dFf3AOgYdjug2rt8/4ly/gktkkfEER_aqng1Us6NtAw/h3/h001.GaT0yfmS2TnGDbI1GWNyEYVpQtkvg8dLzMSawCV0LjM)
American Indian Health and Family Services’**** work is essential, especially because colonialism and racism are still impacting Indigenous people across the United States—leading to major disparities in health between Native and non-Native folks.
For example, our Indigenous neighbors suffer from higher rates of diabetes, asthma, and heart disease, and they have up to 3x higher rates of suicide than any other racial/ethnic group in the United States.
**American Indian Health and Family Services reconnects Native Americans and Alaska Natives with community and Indigenous spirituality, which helps reduce suicide risk.**
The nonprofit also provides trauma-informed counseling, culturally competent talking circles, and statewide suicide prevention programs—along with other life-saving community services and medical and behavioral healthcare.
**American Indian Health and Family Services provides services to**** _all_****people in need, regardless of background or financial means. And right now, they’re fundraising to expand to a new campus so they can double their impact.**
Near public transportation, this new campus will serve twice as many people; offer new services like dental care and a pharmacy; and include a youth center, an outdoor garden with a traditional sweat lodge, and space for on-site events like American Indian Health and Family Services’**** annual pow wow.
[**This Thanksgiving, please donate as generously as you can to support American Indian Health and Family Services and begin to address centuries of injustice.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsXu0R1XDWRVJnEMC9_ePsJWbpxxrEDnhV3WcTpCG3Uf41bm2sGs5Mx81SKPHgzVotXxI3mMW5m68IknkI5bVsQQukoAq4WG2Sk4fMt22sg5_t6Ngh4BNjiSY33oas6hxZkZ7WhvIrnelwvgMEuAXC5dAnC2ICF6ej4J_VJhrkmeN6bLPKM8aVBUI0V4_JsjvzI6mO8k2PGb9VzgCCpmXtj1bhbIsPLbhOhL3t62fAkx4pNbBXs9bD2scIxWWyPAjba0ldr5-yOvfMaaAM52mHmrG-P5DvQtJOZMEYOuxuXyCIK7NN99bIFXj594YvZK_7XiP0ftTJ1kPlpnXjoqFR6bjHnfT1PxfBPemXsqWKZol3tUbM5BQ-e7XK3FuH_jeJpIi1sJ--Elh3hQYwBsj2FpKUPoWdzUgRecJJcI_e8lMFbCiYfk0H48cyTN_clyGQ/4ly/gktkkfEER_aqng1Us6NtAw/h4/h001.Oi-rExnBEQgZo503apSbaMQZQi_zE47Ng3u-brXm8pM)

To: dpwillis67@gmail.com
Friend,
As we approach Thanksgiving, now is a crucial time to support Indigenous-led organizations.
**I particularly want to lift up Native-led healthcare because Native Americans face major health disparities compared to non-Natives.**
Centuries of colonial and racist policies have contributed to chronic stress, discrimination, trauma, and poverty—taking a toll on Indigenous people’s health.
This issue is particularly urgent as Trump and Republicans keep cutting food assistance and healthcare, which Native families depend on. These actions also violate the U.S. government’s treaty responsibilities to Tribes, such as providing food and healthcare in exchange for stealing Tribal lands.
Native healthcare providers are stepping up—such as the nonprofit American Indian Health and Family Services, which has strengthened the health and wellbeing of Michigan’s urban Indigenous community for 47 years.
This essential service provider is fundraising right now to keep meeting our community’s growing needs.

Supporting American Indian and Alaska Native families as well as other underserved communities in our region, **American Indian Health and Family Services** **makes sure not to turn anyone away.**
They serve uninsured people by providing discounts and sliding scale fees based on income, and offer transportation as needed to bring people to programs and appointments.
**But American Indian Health and Family Services is limited by their aging facility’s space constraints, so they’re fundraising to build a new campus**.
This new campus is near public transportation and will serve twice as many people; offer new services like dental care and a pharmacy; and include a youth center, an outdoor garden with a traditional sweat lodge, and space for on-site events like their annual pow wow.

Currently, American Indian Health and Family Services provides:
* **Medical care** such as primary care, preventative screenings, maternal health services, pediatric care, and health education including traditional herbal medicine classes
* **Behavioral healthcare** such as mental health services that respect traditional healing approaches and reinforce cultural identity, trauma-informed counseling, substance abuse treatment and recovery support, and statewide suicide prevention outreach
* **Community health and family wellness services** such as nutrition education, fitness and cooking and gardening classes, youth programs, safe community spaces, elder support, cultural workshops, and seasonal ceremonies

With your support, these offerings will expand even further.
While focusing on improving health access and outcomes for American Indians and Alaska Natives in our region, **American Indian Health and Family Services also provides care for**** _all_****people in need** —regardless of ethnicity, nationality, gender, race, religion, age, sexual orientation, or financial means.
[**Please support American Indian Health and Family Services’ essential work with a generous contribution today.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsXu0R1XDWRVJnEMC9_ePsJWbpxxrEDnhV3WcTpCG3Uf41bm2sGs5Mx81SKPHgzVotXxI3mMW5m68IknkI5bVsQfdp3eBDTEi2BdGWHpwLqWhrJR6YCi-DldmNjzfGz3VwVBDFUVbkQUyVDwLEKqdtI9k4usVgXDVB17i1mIq3Ajc2ztONh_GZBhf5kgzjzO8-k6-hNnq_032WvJ3MlXSQqnhJ_wfPP3zV_Mli2KQkxz_AX-ORNWaok7U5-YoBpd-3sBCvL4qNwI_l_rjSr7ro0j-SajAWgtdbhL7SgxZP_DFG0ximKvA52hbt9XyrCo451SlOkq53PzeJ6aGI8lV00Ob6OY11gpuHlNBqFb-CTqphMZ2xLnGnHw0IDiDTT7cMBptFrJT_U63hbArabSl1XP9TqHTGZwj3f0JN7S1_SiKGro3nAAnL5ao90pUpUoaE/4ly/gktkkfEER_aqng1Us6NtAw/h21/h001.ldhcDvxAou68oY5nsvG26eLNU6TeaCucLPED_1fzeB0)
Thank you so much. I’ve been honored to secure $1.5 million for American Indian Health and Family Services through legislation, and to work alongside Native leaders in the fight for environmental and social justice.
Together we’ll continue fighting back against colonialism and racism while pushing the U.S. government to follow through on its responsibilities to Tribes.
In solidarity,
Rashida

clean_body: Native Americans face major health disparities compared to non-Natives. | | | Friend, This Thanksgiving, let’s fight colonialism by supporting Indigenous-led organizations. | For 47 years American Indian Health and Family Services has strengthened the health and wellbeing of Michigan’s urban Indigenous community through high-quality, accessible care that integrates traditional healing and cultural practices. This photo is from one of their annual pow wows: American Indian Health and Family Services’** work is essential, especially because colonialism and racism are still impacting Indigenous people across the United States—leading to major disparities in health between Native and non-Native folks. For example, our Indigenous neighbors suffer from higher rates of diabetes, asthma, and heart disease, and they have up to 3x higher rates of suicide than any other racial/ethnic group in the United States. American Indian Health and Family Services reconnects Native Americans and Alaska Natives with community and Indigenous spirituality, which helps reduce suicide risk. The nonprofit also provides trauma-informed counseling, culturally competent talking circles, and statewide suicide prevention programs—along with other life-saving community services and medical and behavioral healthcare. American Indian Health and Family Services provides services to** allpeople in need, regardless of background or financial means. And right now, they’re fundraising to expand to a new campus so they can double their impact. Near public transportation, this new campus will serve twice as many people; offer new services like dental care and a pharmacy; and include a youth center, an outdoor garden with a traditional sweat lodge, and space for on-site events like American Indian Health and Family Services’** annual pow wow. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately, with 100% going directly to American Indian Health and Family Services | | | | | | | | | Thank you for standing up for health equity and supporting our Native neighbors as well as healthcare for all. To learn more about this incredible service provider, please check out my email from yesterday. In solidarity, Rashida \---------- Forwarded message --------- From: Rashida Tlaib**** Date: Wed, Nov 26, 2025 Subject: Join me in uplifting Native families this Thanksgiving To: dpwillis67@gmail.com Friend, As we approach Thanksgiving, now is a crucial time to support Indigenous-led organizations. I particularly want to lift up Native-led healthcare because Native Americans face major health disparities compared to non-Natives. Centuries of colonial and racist policies have contributed to chronic stress, discrimination, trauma, and poverty—taking a toll on Indigenous people’s health. This issue is particularly urgent as Trump and Republicans keep cutting food assistance and healthcare, which Native families depend on. These actions also violate the U.S. government’s treaty responsibilities to Tribes, such as providing food and healthcare in exchange for stealing Tribal lands. Native healthcare providers are stepping up—such as the nonprofit American Indian Health and Family Services, which has strengthened the health and wellbeing of Michigan’s urban Indigenous community for 47 years. This essential service provider is fundraising right now to keep meeting our community’s growing needs. This Native-led, Detroit-based nonprofit health and wellness center provides high-quality, accessible, and holistic care that integrates traditional healing and cultural practices. Here’s a photo from one of their community pow wows: Supporting American Indian and Alaska Native families as well as other underserved communities in our region, American Indian Health and Family Services makes sure not to turn anyone away. They serve uninsured people by providing discounts and sliding scale fees based on income, and offer transportation as needed to bring people to programs and appointments. But American Indian Health and Family Services is limited by their aging facility’s space constraints, so they’re fundraising to build a new campus. This new campus is near public transportation and will serve twice as many people; offer new services like dental care and a pharmacy; and include a youth center, an outdoor garden with a traditional sweat lodge, and space for on-site events like their annual pow wow. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately, with 100% going directly to American Indian Health and Family Services | | | | | | | | | Currently, American Indian Health and Family Services provides: Medical care such as primary care, preventative screenings, maternal health services, pediatric care, and health education including traditional herbal medicine classes Behavioral healthcare such as mental health services that respect traditional healing approaches and reinforce cultural identity, trauma-informed counseling, substance abuse treatment and recovery support, and statewide suicide prevention outreach Community health and family wellness services such as nutrition education, fitness and cooking and gardening classes, youth programs, safe community spaces, elder support, cultural workshops, and seasonal ceremonies With your support, these offerings will expand even further. While focusing on improving health access and outcomes for American Indians and Alaska Natives in our region, American Indian Health and Family Services also provides care for allpeople in need —regardless of ethnicity, nationality, gender, race, religion, age, sexual orientation, or financial means. Thank you so much. I’ve been honored to secure $1.5 million for American Indian Health and Family Services through legislation, and to work alongside Native leaders in the fight for environmental and social justice. Together we’ll continue fighting back against colonialism and racism while pushing the U.S. government to follow through on its responsibilities to Tribes. Happy Thanksgiving!

I had to take a moment to tell you how thankful I am to have you on this team. With your support, we continue to build a new political home in West Michigan.

I also want to say how thankful I am to my biggest supporters: my husband Jesse and our sons, James and Wesley. I could not be here without them by my side.

I hope your day is filled with gratitude, great food, and loved ones.

Grateful,

Hillary

name: Seth Magaziner
subject: Happy Thanksgiving!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY Magaziner for Congress
body:
Seth is in Congress to fight for the middle class and those working hard to join it. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://act.sethmagaziner.com/p/9_n7o1XMskam_GoKXFDAdg2?unsubscribedata=0ZZEJm7U%2F6WMEzO%2FksMxYrIusfyUbf5zjBWceaL5V0v1LlzIWvTVjb6bVPUaRXLw2lGAj%2FL%2F%2F06WyuMIxUtCjrb6315mFS3IIzeRgi5xS30XyU7HCbCrdtTMEXxxgWf6Rxk9alrbXWnb9HgC%2BXX8csDm%2FIEEgnDcETrNcO8xQYFGaabJzIdAICTu0o8WmHgTWsKtf46vifTaM5kb%2B0YyNhRtlbyGEUZ5zSgevA7FPiN6QldDk1Ld7W%2FccxLYy3kh1Bwz%2Bsl4uWcCJa7AdCeXyHk%2FAozQ5bY8jPtRSzKvyte3iOfOCFc8beatpOCokOOHVioa6MymV8nD4JZOT4NA8wbi2QkU2LMOD1HBttqOmC8NBSPH9QbnGhbkGUQjW1yrfIKZ9qBZVqKruO2AWOyn3bHcJ3gMaUFSnpdE5EcdwxZabiZVGmECh4cdNlqlx796)

| |
---
Peter,

This Thanksgiving, I am incredibly thankful for you and all of my supporters. My fight in Washington is always in service to hardworking Rhode Islanders, and I’m honored to be your voice and Representative.

Our fight for a more affordable future in RI-02 continues. But today, let us pause and reflect on the things we are grateful for.

I hope you are able to spend time with and connect with your loved ones (and of course, eat some amazing food).

From my family to yours, Happy Thanksgiving!

Seth

name: Christina Bohannan
subject: Have a wonderful Thanksgiving!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CHRISTINA BOHANNAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120104566/585243244/-975735282?refcode=em_logoheader&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNDhiNTMxMS1hOGNiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qVDWTAz2sSEFwa83zfQ_lHPByqKUjFm939lznwSD9GE=)

Peter, Happy Thanksgiving!

Before the cooking begins and the day fills with the usual swirl of chaos, I wanted to pause for a moment and tell you how deeply grateful I am for your support in this campaign. Politics can feel exhausting; especially in moments like these. But knowing I can count on you is a reminder that none of us are in this alone. We’re choosing, together, to believe in a better tomorrow.

Thanksgiving has always been one of my favorite days because it brings me back to the heart of what matters. Growing up, we didn’t have much. But every year, both my grandmas and all my cousins would pack into our double-wide trailer, squeezing around a kitchen table that somehow always managed to fit everyone. My mom would be up at 4 a.m. making the full spread — turkey, stuffing, collard greens, pan-baked cornbread, acre peas, and my favorite... _chocolate cream pie._

What we lacked in space or money, we made up for in love. No matter what struggles were weighing on us, once we were gathered around that table, we were reminded that we had each other.

These days, too many families in our district are carrying heavy worries of their own. They’re doing their best and still feeling left behind by leaders who look away when times get tough. I'm fighting to bring you and your neighbors peace of mind in a district where we can all flourish together. I am endlessly grateful that you have chosen to support this vision for what our community could be.

I hope you and your family share a wonderful Thanksgiving.

— Christina

---

name: Alexandra Rojas
subject: Because of you
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Hi Peter,

Before many log off to spend Thanksgiving with family and friends, I
wanted to connect with you directly. I am deeply grateful for the support
that makes our work possible and for the shared commitment to building a
better future together.

To every person who nominated a candidate, talked to us about your
community, gave $5 or more to one of our grassroots campaigns, and helped
us build our movement every day — THANK YOU!

Justice Democrats was founded to build people power in our country by
finding and electing a bloc of working class leaders to Congress who
reject corporate PAC money and truly put people first.

Heading into the new year, momentum is on our side. Candidates powered by
the working class like Zohran are defying the odds. Voters are sick and
tired of the status quo, and no longer believe the leadership that got us
here is capable of leading us out of it.

Because of you, we have already endorsed five new working-class,
progressive challengers:

* Donavan McKinney in MI-13: a lifelong working class Detroiter and
State Representative, who has led the charge in Michigan to take out
corporate money in politics and deliver clean air & water, challenging
out-of-touch millionaire Rep. Shri Thanedar.
* Angela Gonzales-Torres in CA-34: a working-class Angeleno and daughter
of a deportee, dedicated community advocate, and public servant taking
on corporate Rep. Jimmy Gomez, who was one of the biggest recipients
of AIPAC’s Super PAC spending last year.
* Cori Bush in MO-01: our first-ever Justice Democrat, a nurse,
community activist, and former progressive champion in Congress — who
has put her body on the line for working class people — running to
take back her seat from AIPAC darling Rep. Wesley Bell.
* Justin J. Pearson in TN-09: a member of the Tennessee Three, State
Representative, and environmental justice organizer, who stopped
multiple Big Oil pipelines, challenging Do-Nothing Democrat Rep. Steve
Cohen.
* Darializa Avila Chevalier in NY-13: a working-class Afro-Latina
organizer, UAW member, and daughter of Dominican immigrants — who has
organized her community to defend against ICE, subway fare hikes, and
the genocide in Gaza — going up against AIPAC and real estate
industry-backed Rep. Adriano Espaillat.

And even more are on the way!

We wouldn’t be here without you,
Peter, as we depend directly on grassroots
contributions from people like you to fuel the work ahead.

I and every member of the Justice Democrats team are so incredibly
grateful for you. Together, we CAN drive big money out of politics and
elect representatives of the people to create lasting change in
Washington.

Thank you again for anything you’ve contributed to strengthen and grow our
working-class, progressive movement in Congress. Our work is just getting
started.

In solidarity,

Alexandra Rojas
Executive Director

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20251127-37231-footer&t=1002&refcode2=37231_1203867_WnfxIU&akid=37231%2E1203867%2EWnfxIU

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1003&akid=37231%2E1203867%2EWnfxIU

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
Justice Democrats
1390 Columbia Ave #261
Lancaster PA 17603
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.

**Behind the Markets**
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

clean_body: Elon Musk just humiliated Donald Trump in front of millions — live on X .| | | | November 27 | | | | | Trump Hits Back After Musk’s Brutal Tweetstorm | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. name: Ro Khanna
subject: Happy Thanksgiving!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ro for Congress
body:
https://rokhanna.com [https://rokhanna.com]
Peter,
Every year, the holiday season reminds us of something fundamental: we’re at our best when we’re showing up for one another.
This week marked my third year in a row of joining Commissioner William McCurdy II and local community leaders to deliver over 600 turkeys and trimmings to families across Clark County, helping ensure every family can gather around a full table on Thanksgiving.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/103205/images/image.jpeg]
What always strikes me most on days like this is the spirit of generosity that defines our country.
No matter our politics or disagreements, we are bound together by a shared belief in community, dignity, and taking care of our neighbors.
From my family to yours, I want to wish you a safe, healthy, and happy Thanksgiving. I hope the days ahead bring you rest, joy, and time with the people you love.
Onward,
Ro
Wait! One more thing–
Ro doesn't accept a cent from lobbyists or PACs, meaning our work is completely funded by grassroots supporters like you. By donating just $3 today, you’re helping us create a country that works for everyone, not just those at the top. That means a renewed investment in American manufacturing, Medicare for All, student debt relief, and a green future.
If you want to support our people-powered campaign as we continue to fight for progress in Washington, please consider making a donation of $3 or more today. Thank you!

name: Ken Martin
subject: This Thanksgiving:
body:
---
Derek, As we get ready to celebrate Thanksgiving with family and friends, I want to express my gratitude for your support. With your help, Democrats have won or overperformed in nearly 90 percent of key races this year. That didn’t just happen by itself. It happened because supporters like you have stepped up, dedicated your time, and powered a Democratic resurgence across the country. Thank you for being a part of this work. I hope you’re able to take time this Thanksgiving to rest, recharge, and connect with loved ones and your community. We have a lot of work ahead of us. Thank you, Ken Ken Martin
Chair
Democratic National Committee | [](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-november-2025?refcode=em_20251127_5374_dc&amount=7&refcode2=5374_22930333_lb8IyA&akid=5374%2E22930333%2Elb8IyA)
---

_If you gave recently via check or mail, our system may not have yet processed your gift. We are appreciative and thank you greatly for your support._

name: Tom Kean (personal)
subject: Happy Thanksgiving
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kean for Congress Inc _Copyright © 2025 Kean for Congress_ **Our mailing address is:**
body:
From my family to yours...| | | Friend, From my family to yours, **Happy Thanksgiving! ** In reflecting on the many things I’m thankful for this year, one of the things I’m especially grateful for is your trust and the privilege of representing you in Congress. It is a true honor to work on your behalf and serve the people of New Jersey every day. That is something I will never take for granted. Enjoy your day today. clean_body: From my family to yours...| | | Friend, From my family to yours, Happy Thanksgiving! In reflecting on the many things I’m thankful for this year, one of the things I’m especially grateful for is your trust and the privilege of representing you in Congress. It is a true honor to work on your behalf and serve the people of New Jersey every day. That is something I will never take for granted. Enjoy your day today. I hope you get the chance to slow down, look around, and take in all the blessings that surround you. With gratitude, | | | | Tom Kean, Jr. | | Paid for by Kean for Congress Inc Copyright © 2025 Kean for Congress Our mailing address is: Kean for Congress Inc PO Box 999 Edison, NJ 08818 Click to mail your donation. name: Binkley for Congress
subject: Grateful for You This Thanksgiving
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ryan Binkley for Congress
body:
| | | | | |
---
| |
Friend ,
As we gather with family and loved ones this Thanksgiving, I want to take a moment to share my gratitude with you.
This year has reminded me just how blessed we are as a community and as a nation. Even in challenging times, God continues to guide us, strengthen us, and call us to serve one another with compassion and courage.
I’m especially thankful for the encouragement, prayers, and partnership so many of you have offered. Your support means more than you know. You remind me every day why I believe so deeply in the people of North Texas and in the promise of our country.
As you sit around the table this week, whether it’s a big family gathering or a quiet dinner at home, I pray that joy, peace, and hope fill your hearts. May we remember the blessings we share, the freedoms we cherish, and the opportunities ahead to make a difference together.
From my family to yours, Happy Thanksgiving. May God continue to bless you and guide our nation.
With gratitude,Ryan
---
| | | | | | | | | | | | | | |

name: Ron Wyden
subject: Thankful and Hopeful
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by Wyden for Senate
body:
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120106475/585249498/2140832878?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTQxMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNWFhOTcyNy1hYWNiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8_wuPQUmKHV8FRmH78lSXpDZeifTdgsM_x6Pb--hZBU=&emci=26cd1628-c5c9-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=05aa9727-aacb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=9818364)
---

| | I’m eternally thankful for our grassroots movement of hard-working Oregonians who work day in and day out to create a better America. **And this year, I am more thankful than ever for your support.** As our country has faced immense challenges this year, I’m inspired by all those who refuse to give in to fear, all who stand steadfast to defend our core values, and all who continue stepping up to create positive change in their communities. **It is because of you that I remain hopeful for future generations – and I know that our work together has never been more important.** I remain dedicated to protecting liberty and justice for all, and creating paths to opportunity for everyone. So to all of you who have partnered with me in this work and remain dedicated to the work ahead, I thank you. Wishing you and yours a very Happy Thanksgiving. Onward, _Ron_ |
---|---|---

| | Paid for and authorized by Wyden for Senate.

name: Heidi Campbell
subject: Happy Thanksgiving
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Campbell for State Senate, Lee Jones, treasurer
body:
https://voteheidicampbell.com/ [https://voteheidicampbell.com/]
Peter,
Happy Thanksgiving to you and your family!
I am thankful to live in a community with so many wonderful people who regularly step up to fight for what is right, help those less fortunate, and don’t back down in the face of challenges or hard times. I couldn’t do the work I do in the State Senate without this community.
https://voteheidicampbell.com/ [https://voteheidicampbell.com/]
I hope you have a good holiday and can spend time with the ones you love
Thank you!
Heidi Campbell
State Senator
Senator Heidi Campbell is a Tennessee State Senator, representing District 20. She is championing change for Tennessee and won't rest until we see gun safety reform, corporate accountability, and a safe and prosperous community where everyone can thrive.

name: Juan Ciscomani
subject: thankful + grateful
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CISCOMANI FOR CONGRESS
body:
Wishing you a wonderful Thanksgiving holiday. |
---
| | [ ](https://juanciscomani.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4ed32a393c9fe1089f10fee94&id=92bf352c30&e=659fba240e)

# **Happy Thanksgiving**
From our family to yours, Happy Thanksgiving! 🦃🍂 Laura and I are feeling especially grateful today for the privilege of serving Arizona and for the incredible people who call our community home. Whether you’re traveling, gathering with loved ones, or finding a quiet moment of rest, we hope today is filled with gratitude, laughter, and connection. Thank you for your trust, your prayers, and your continued support.

— Juan & Laura Ciscomani

name: Andy Roth
subject: Happy Thanksgiving!
body:
| | | [](http://give.statefreedomcaucus.org/ls/click?upn=u001.SSCAglCEkhp55PDWKhWTjk18vO7wfT-2FWuWGj5qYFNqYx7X5ohQ4dCOx5p85so2pLt73s_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVBZyx1SQGGYhOdwcjZYtHerBqZoGlFF9RT1hxLLrxUxRKgJXpiIZmcFW-2BWySXWSWeysSIbz9V-2FWBDdTZF0uttC8veZ2FSCRiQdwfFLVDv2VIx-2FIqbae9FowKy4H4zOmaOSKDBF5xvMpMkW8iMBCp11DnbL9Do9toCqw4FqSmN0YmXUYXok2E1Bj4mJaz6hQ662EpiQFnh2ZUmD6QZl2pWy-2F46I7FqwV-2BByayj-2BapgNKiVeYRJ3FABpyizp5Plf-2FHqg-3D)
---
|
---
Dear Friend, As we gather with family and friends this week, I wanted to take a moment to say how grateful we are for your steadfast support of the State Freedom Caucus Network. Our work to defend liberty in every state could not be done without you.

This season reminds us of the first Thanksgiving, when a faithful people gave thanks to God for His providence and the freedom to live according to their convictions. Today, we continue in that same spirit, grateful for the gift of this nation, our families, and the freedom that allows us to serve God and our country.

From all of us at SFCN, may your homes be filled with peace, gratitude, and the joy of His countless blessings.

Wishing you and your family a happy Thanksgiving,
---
---
---
Andy RothPresidentState Freedom Caucus Network
---

name: Jena Griswold
subject: Thanksgiving wishes
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jena for Colorado
body:
https://jenaforcolorado.com/ [https://jenaforcolorado.com/]Happy Thanksgiving, Peter!
Whether you’re watching the Thanksgiving Day Parade, enjoying the dishes and spending time with loved ones, or taking the day to relax, I hope today kicks off a season full of warmth and gratitude.
As I reflect on the year, I am thankful for my wonderful husband, our little toddler, and our entire family. I am also grateful for the support received in this Attorney General’s race. Running for office is no easy feat, but with the support of this team, we have already been able to accomplish so much. The opportunity to continue serving the people of Colorado is an honor I will never take for granted.
Wishing you a wonderful day!
Jena Griswold
Follow Jena Online:

clean_body: [ Thanksgiving, Peter! Whether you’re watching the Thanksgiving Day Parade, enjoying the dishes and spending time with loved ones, or taking the day to relax, I hope today kicks off a season full of warmth and gratitude. As I reflect on the year, I am thankful for my wonderful husband, our little toddler, and our entire family. I am also grateful for the support received in this Attorney General’s race. Running for office is no easy feat, but with the support of this team, we have already been able to accomplish so much. The opportunity to continue serving the people of Colorado is an honor I will never take for granted. Wishing you a wonderful day! name: Adam Schiff
subject: Please share some of your Thanksgiving bounty
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
|
# [](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/61?t=1&akid=9244%2E5257348%2E9CEiKF)
This Thanksgiving, I’m thinking about those who can’t fill their plates, Derek.
More than five million Californians, including children and seniors, rely on food assistance.
That’s every single day — not just on Thanksgiving.
[](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=nov_abs_em_foodbank.HigKpI&refcode=AU_ABS_CL_FOT_GEN_AC90_20251127_AM1_V1_S1&t=4&akid=9244%2E5257348%2E9CEiKF)
_Earlier this month, I stopped by Jimmy Kimmel’s Big Beautiful Food Bank to help keep food on Californians’ tables._
Our communities have been struggling with rising food costs and disastrous cuts to nutrition assistance. First, because of the GOP’s Big Ugly Bill. Later, because of the GOP government shutdown, when Trump refused to provide funding for SNAP benefits (ignoring judicial orders at that).
**[This Thanksgiving, I’m asking you to join me in contributing to the LA Regional Food Bank, the San Francisco-Marin Food Bank, and the Sacramento Food Bank and Family Services. 100% of your donation will go toward ending hunger and helping families across the Golden State.](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=nov_abs_em_foodbank.HigKpI&refcode=AU_ABS_CL_FOT_GEN_AC90_20251127_AM1_V1_S1&t=5&akid=9244%2E5257348%2E9CEiKF)**
[DONATE](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=nov_abs_em_foodbank.HigKpI&refcode=AU_ABS_CL_FOT_GEN_AC90_20251127_AM1_V1_S1&email=&t=6&akid=9244%2E5257348%2E9CEiKF)

Your continued support and generosity mean the world to me. And will help feed some needy families this Holiday season.
Happy Thanksgiving.

name: LeftNet
subject: 47.4 million people live in food-insecure households
body:
[1]LeftNet

As many families gather around full tables this Thanksgiving, millions
across the country are facing a very different reality. USDA data shows
that 47.4 million people live in food-insecure households, meaning they
struggled to afford enough food at some point during the year.

This year, the Trump administration announced it will discontinue the food
insecurity survey, and some experts have stated that ending the survey
will make it harder to track and understand the scale of food insecurity.
Without the annual report, non-profit organizations and policymakers won't
have a clear lens on the scale of hunger in America and how to prevent it.

With the rise of food insecurity in our country, organizations like
Feeding America and its network of more than 200 food banks and 60,000
pantries, and meal programs have stood on the front lines making sure
families do not go hungry. Their work is even more critical now,
especially at a time when many families are struggling with rising food
prices and changes to nutrition-assistance programs that happened just
last month.

If you’re looking for a meaningful way to give back this Thanksgiving,
we’re encouraging you to take action. Some ways you can help families are
by:

1. [ https://act.leftnet.org/go/21450?c0=leftnet_em20251127feedingamerica.gRdPZc&refcode=em20251127-1374&amount=5&t=1001&akid=1374%2E2118727%2E7sqGml ]Make a donation to Feeding America to help fight food insecurity
2. Volunteer at your local food bank — you can find your local food bank
[ https://act.leftnet.org/go/22085?source=em20251127-1374&t=1002&akid=1374%2E2118727%2E7sqGml ]here
3. Check drop-off hours and most-needed items
4. Donate food, host a drive, or volunteer your time if you’re able

A Thanksgiving meal may seem small, but for a family struggling to stretch
groceries or a child facing empty cupboards, it can bring a moment of
joy.

Thanks for taking a moment to think of others this season. Wishing you and
your loved ones a warm and meaningful Thanksgiving 🦃

— LeftNet

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.leftnet.org/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=1374%2E2118727%2E7sqGml

name: Jim
subject: Please, send help
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the New Hampshire Republican State Committee, Chairman Jim MacEachern
body:
My blue-haired niece is home from her first semester of college and has decided to give the entire family a lecture...

| | | | | [|
---](https://gop.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6abc8216f2c93cdbede56dda3&id=56a8d54894&e=f3aa6419cd)
| | | | | |

Friend,**Send help.** I’m trapped at Thanksgiving dinner... and it’s getting worse by the minute.My blue-haired niece is home from her first semester of college and has decided to give the entire family a lecture on “late-stage capitalism,” “tax justice,” and “why New Hampshire _needs_ an income tax if it wants to be taken seriously.”She hasn’t taken a single economics class, but she’s armed with a podcast and unlimited confidence.My cousin looked at me across the table like a man stranded on a lifeboat.There’s only _**one**_ way to prove her wrong before she starts explaining why ICE should be abolished over the turkey:**Hit our Thanksgiving fundraising goal before dessert.** If we can show her that Granite Staters are stepping up to defend low taxes, public safety, and the Live Free or Die way of life... I might actually make it to pie.But the clock is ticking. She’s already moved on to student loan forgiveness and “why New Hampshire should study what California is doing.”[ _**Please — for the love of sanity, stuffing, and this great state — chip in HERE right now:**_](https://gop.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6abc8216f2c93cdbede56dda3&id=059c193b19&e=f3aa6419cd)
---
| [SAVE ME](https://gop.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6abc8216f2c93cdbede56dda3&id=7e6562bc98&e=f3aa6419cd)
---
| [PROVE HER WRONG](https://gop.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6abc8216f2c93cdbede56dda3&id=fa961d6234&e=f3aa6419cd)
---
| [DEFEND NEW HAMPSHIRE](https://gop.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6abc8216f2c93cdbede56dda3&id=52d7afbbaf&e=f3aa6419cd)
---
| |

Thank you — and Happy Thanksgiving from the NHGOP.May your turkey be tender and your political arguments mercifully brief.— Chairman Jim MacEachern

name: Lt. Col. David Flippo
subject: Happy Thanksgiving, Nevada!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by David Flippo for Nevada]( "Paid for by David Flippo for Nevada")
body:
I hope you and your family have a blessed Thanksgiving! |
---
| | [ ](https://electdavidflippo.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9aba26b775663b53df718fc1a&id=77e0c9de18&e=273c9a71fb)
---
| | Peter ,

As we gather with family and friends this Thanksgiving, I would like to take a moment to express my heartfelt gratitude to you.

Thanksgiving is more than a holiday; **it is a reminder to pause, reflect, and appreciate the blessings we share as Nevadans and as Americans.** Whether around a crowded table or a quiet one, this day reminds us of the strength we find in community and the values that unite us.
---
|
---
| This year, I am especially thankful for the countless men and women who serve our nation at home and abroad. Many will spend this holiday far from their families so that our families can celebrate in peace. Their sacrifice, and the sacrifices of their loved ones, are never forgotten. I am also grateful for the spirit of our communities across Nevada’s 4th District. From our small towns to our growing neighborhoods, I see resilience, generosity, and the same love of country that inspired me to serve so many years ago.

As you celebrate today, I hope your home is filled with warmth, good food, and the company of people who mean the most to you. May this season bring you rest, joy, and renewed hope for the year ahead.

>From my family to yours, Happy Thanksgiving! May God continue to bless our great state and this great nation.
---
| For Nevada, David Flippo
Republican Candidate for U.S. Congress
Nevada’s 4th Congressional District
**[www.Flippo4NV.com](https://electdavidflippo.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9aba26b775663b53df718fc1a&id=155713fe88&e=273c9a71fb)**

clean_body: I hope you and your family have a blessed Thanksgiving! | | | | | Peter , As we gather with family and friends this Thanksgiving, I would like to take a moment to express my heartfelt gratitude to you. Thanksgiving is more than a holiday; it is a reminder to pause, reflect, and appreciate the blessings we share as Nevadans and as Americans. Whether around a crowded table or a quiet one, this day reminds us of the strength we find in community and the values that unite us. | | This year, I am especially thankful for the countless men and women who serve our nation at home and abroad. Many will spend this holiday far from their families so that our families can celebrate in peace. Their sacrifice, and the sacrifices of their loved ones, are never forgotten. I am also grateful for the spirit of our communities across Nevada’s 4th District. From our small towns to our growing neighborhoods, I see resilience, generosity, and the same love of country that inspired me to serve so many years ago. As you celebrate today, I hope your home is filled with warmth, good food, and the company of people who mean the most to you. May this season bring you rest, joy, and renewed hope for the year ahead. From my family to yours, Happy Thanksgiving! May God continue to bless our great state and this great nation. | For Nevada, David Flippo Republican Candidate for U.S. Congress Nevada’s 4th Congressional District | | | | | | | | | | | | | | "Paid for by David Flippo for Nevada" | Copyright © 2025 Flippo for Nevada, All rights reserved.

name: Pramila Jayapal
subject: Happy Thanksgiving
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
On this day of gratitude, it means a lot to me to know that you’re with us in the fight to defend our democracy.
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20251127-5658-footer&t=1&akid=5658%2E420207%2E-7zMVr)
Hi Peter,
I wanted to send a quick note to wish you a happy Thanksgiving. This year has been tough for all of us—but now more than ever, I’m grateful for everyone who has chosen to join us in nonviolent resistance. On this day of gratitude, it means a lot to me to know that you’re with us in the fight to defend our democracy.
So I hope you have a restful holiday and a chance to eat a delicious Thanksgiving meal. There will be more work to come before the end of the year, but for now, enjoy the holiday and spend some quality time with your loved ones. You deserve it.
In gratitude,
Pramila Jayapal

name: John James
subject: Happy Thanksgiving!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by John James for MI, 35744 Van Dyke Avenue, Sterling Heights, MI
body:
Friend,

>From my family to yours, I want to wish you a very happy Thanksgiving.

Today, I am thankful for the men and women in uniform who keep this nation
protected. I am thankful for the military families who hold our communities
together. And I am thankful for every American who believes this nation is
still worth protecting.

We have the freedom to worship, to work, to raise our families, and to shape
the future of this great nation. Those blessings are worth defending.

I am deeply grateful for your support and your faith in our mission. None of
what we are fighting for would be possible without you.

Thank you for standing with me, not just today, but every day we work to keep
Michigan strong.

With gratitude,
John James

48312

name: Jay Feely
subject: Let's give thanks.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Feely for Congress
body:
| | | Happy Thanksgiving! Today, I’m especially grateful to live in the greatest country in history. As we gather around the table, I’m reminded of how blessed we are as Americans and how fortunate I am to have supporters and friends like you. This Thanksgiving, I hope your home is filled with warmth, good food, and the people who mean the most to you. My family and I are giving thanks for you today, and praying that this holiday brings peace, joy, and a little bit of football to yours! **\- Jay Feely**

name: Michael Bennet
subject: I can't thank you enough, friend
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for Governor
body:
Wishing you a delicious meal, a restful food coma and a joyful holiday full of love and gratitude. Thank you for all that you do.
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120112484/585268102/-2054079050?refcode=jtk986-ot-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NGJlZTYzYS1iM2NiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=30qF8mxU5E6q8O0tPAKSJFd6JNlEUD15mkupKGkwNvg=)
---
Michael here, friends – and I couldn't be more thankful to spend Thanksgiving with my family (including Bandit and Captain Flint, too).
I've always been in charge of fixing the Thanksgiving bird. And if you need a new twist on your standard turkey recipe, I _highly recommend_ you try my **infamous turkey vindaloo recipe:**
This twist on Madhur Jaffrey’s chicken vindaloo has become a Bennet family tradition for Thanksgiving – and in the years I get it right (never guaranteed), it is fall-off-the-bone good.
But as we come to the end of a whirlwind year spent fighting for Coloradans in Washington as I work to earn your vote for governor back here at home, I wanted to take a moment to express my deepest gratitude to the people who make it all possible – like you, friend.
**Thank you** for giving your time, money, and energy in support of our shared responsibility to build opportunity for one another and for the next generation.
**Thank you** for your commitment to doing everything you can to help us make Colorado the best place to live, work, and raise a family.
**And above all, thank you for believing in me.**
However you choose to have your turkey today, I hope you enjoy a delicious meal, a restful food coma, and a joyful holiday with the people you love the most. I know I will.
Thank you again, and Happy Thanksgiving,

– Michael and the entire Bennet family
| Bennet for Governor
PO Box 270117
Louisville, CO 80027
United States **Paid for by Bennet for Governor. name: Squad Victory Fund
subject: So much to be grateful for — and it starts with you
body:
[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/18910?refcode=em20251127-1657-foote&t=1001&akid=1657%2E342411%2EB0kIS5 ]Squad Victory Fund

Peter,

As we take a moment to reflect, our hearts are full. Not because the
challenges are small — they aren’t. But because you have shown, again and
again, what a people-powered movement can do when we refuse to back down.

The Squad Victory Fund is grounded in a simple truth: real change comes
from the grassroots. Whether you chipped in a few dollars, volunteered
your time, organized in your community, or simply kept believing in a more
just and equitable country, you strengthened this movement.

We’re grateful for every single supporter who said: our communities
deserve better.

Because of your commitment, Alexandria, Ayanna, Ilhan, and Rashida are
able to keep serving the people who sent them to Congress — working-class
families who deserve a government that finally works for them, not for
corporate interests or the political establishment.

[1]Image of Alexandria, Ayanna, Ilhan, and Rashida

And because of you, we’re continuing the fight every day — for living
wages, climate justice, immigrant families, reproductive freedom, and a
democracy that actually belongs to the people. This movement is at its
strongest when the grassroots leads the way, and you prove that truth
constantly.

In a political moment defined by division and greed, you’re building
something radically different: solidarity, community, and a future rooted
in dignity, justice, and care for one another.

We’re grateful — deeply and unapologetically — for your support. You show
us what’s possible when ordinary people step up with extraordinary
courage.

Thank you for being the backbone of this movement. We could not do this
without you.

With gratitude and solidarity,

The Squad Victory Fund

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe:

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.squadvictoryfund.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=1657%2E342411%2EB0kIS5

Paid for by The Squad Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

In addition to what’s legally required, The Squad Victory Fund does not accept contributions from: (1) corporations, associations, or their PACs; (2) individuals registered as federal lobbyists or under FARA; (3) from the PACs, lobbyists, or SEC-named executives of fossil fuel companies; or (4) from the Fraternal Order of Police.

Contributions or gifts to The Squad Victory Fund are not tax deductible.

Contributions to The Squad Victory Fund will be allocated equally among: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

Any contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if any contributor makes a contribution that, when allocated, would exceed the amount that the contributor may lawfully give to any participant.

After contributing to The Squad Victory Fund, you will automatically be signed up to receive email updates from Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, and Rashida Tlaib for Congress. You may of course opt-out by unsubscribing at any time.

To contribute via check, please address to The Squad Victory Fund c/o MBA Consulting Group, PO Box 15845, Washington D.C., 20003

Email us: info@squadvictoryfund.com

But because you have shown, again and again, what a people-powered movement can do when we refuse to back down. The Squad Victory Fund is grounded in a simple truth: real change comes from the grassroots. Whether you chipped in a few dollars, volunteered your time, organized in your community, or simply kept believing in a more just and equitable country, you strengthened this movement. We’re grateful for every single supporter who said: our communities deserve better. Because of your commitment, Alexandria, Ayanna, Ilhan, and Rashida are able to keep serving the people who sent them to Congress — working-class families who deserve a government that finally works for them, not for corporate interests or the political establishment. [1]Image of Alexandria, Ayanna, Ilhan, and Rashida And because of you, we’re continuing the fight every day — for living wages, climate justice, immigrant families, reproductive freedom, and a democracy that actually belongs to the people. This movement is at its strongest when the grassroots leads the way, and you prove that truth constantly. In a political moment defined by division and greed, you’re building something radically different: solidarity, community, and a future rooted in dignity, justice, and care for one another. We’re grateful — deeply and unapologetically — for your support. You show us what’s possible when ordinary people step up with extraordinary courage. Thank you for being the backbone of this movement. We could not do this without you. With gratitude and solidarity, The Squad Victory Fund
