subject: a short note from our family to yours
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Susie Lee for Congress
body:
|
---

| | Hey there, Team Lee, it’s Susie. I couldn’t log off for the evening before wishing you a very happy Thanksgiving! I'm thankful for so much this year: the greatest kids in the world; supportive friends; a big, loving family (seven siblings and lots of nieces and nephews); and a job I love, which gives me purpose every single day. And this year, I’m especially grateful to you, friend. You stood with me during the toughest election of my career, and above all, you’ve entrusted me with the awesome responsibility of representing our community and the values we hold dear. **Thank you for everything.** I know you’ve probably got a lot on your plate (pun intended!) preparing for tomorrow’s festivities, so I won’t keep you any longer. With that, Nevada, Cody, Teton, and I wish you a warm, wonderful, safe, and happy Thanksgiving! With love & gratitude,
Susie Lee

| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

name: Josh Gottheimer
subject: Happy Thanksgiving!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOSH GOTTHEIMER FOR CONGRESS
body:
# [#] I just wanted to reach out and wish you and your family a Happy Thanksgiving!

This has been a long year, but I am thankful for all of the progress we have made toward a brighter future, not just in New Jersey, but across the country.

I’m thankful for you. Without grassroots supporters like you, we wouldn’t be able to fight to protect our Jersey Values. I’m grateful for the chance to keep on fighting for you and tackling the big problems we face as a nation.

And most of all, we must come together and remember to be thankful for our Jersey communities. Every one of our neighbors deserves family, friends, and plenty of joy (and turkey)!

Thank you so much for your continued support, and I hope you have a happy and safe holiday.

Yours,

Josh Gottheimer
Josh Gottheimer represents New Jersey’s Fifth Congressional District. Josh is in Congress to fight for lower taxes and Jersey Values. He’s working across the aisle to find real solutions for New Jersey families and is actively fighting against the ideological gridlock in Washington. name: Haley Stevens
subject: Just a little gratitude, from me to you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate
body:
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=1&akid=1490%2E49239%2EQO2cqM)
| | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/9940?t=2&akid=1490%2E49239%2EQO2cqM)
---
Hi null,

Happy Thanksgiving! 🧡 I hope today finds you surrounded by love, laughter, and something delicious right out of the oven.
Before you sit down with your family, I wanted to take a moment to say thank you.

Running for Senate is one of the most meaningful, humbling things I’ve ever done. And I truly mean this when I say: I wouldn’t be here without you. Whether you’ve known me since I was a little girl growing up in Michigan, or the auto rescue days, or just joined our grassroots team, I am so grateful to be in this with you.
I think about the families I’ve met all over Michigan – from the diners to union halls to school cafeterias – and I carry your stories with me every day. They remind me why I do this work. Why I get up early, stay up late, and fight like heck for the people who make this state the beating heart of America.
I love Michigan. I love Michiganders. And I love fighting for you.

Thank you for believing in me. Thank you for standing up for our democracy, our values, and each other.

I’m so grateful for you, null.
Wishing you a joyful, restful holiday.

With grit, gratitude, and gravy,

Haley

**Behind the Markets**
Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: What I’m grateful for this year
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for Senate
body:
We’re still fighting back. And we will win.

| | | [ ](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/216?t=3&akid=2718%2E4032634%2EJlM_mF)
---
Happy Thanksgiving. This year has been one of incredible challenges and terrifying new realities. As I reflect today, I find so much to feel grateful for, even as we have been tested time and again. I’m grateful for every single person who has kept up the fight to build an America that reflects our best values. For the organizations and groups of volunteers that stepped up in the wake of the government shutdown to support communities. For the Americans who are taking a stand against the GOP’s unprecedented mid-decade gerrymandering attempts. For the people defending their neighbors who are being disappeared by ICE. For the voters across the country who cast their ballots for candidates who demonstrated we CAN make our economy more affordable and fairer for everyone. For the volunteers and staffers who supported this year’s campaigns — knocking on doors, making calls, talking with their families, taking a leap of faith, and pouring themselves into a movement they believed in. For the millions and millions of people across the country who showed up to declare loudly and clearly: We have No Kings in America. For the workers demanding better conditions for themselves and their colleagues. For my family, Bruce, and Bailey (and our walks around the pond). And I’m grateful for everyone on this team — you — and our shared belief in dreaming big, fighting hard, and making big, structural change together. We’re not accepting defeat. We’re still fighting back. And we will win. Wherever you are for the holiday, I hope the food is good and the company is warm. Remember, everyone should do their fair share: bring a dish, set the table, clean up — or, for any billionaires at your table, pay their fair share in taxes. Happy Thanksgiving — and thanks for being a part of this, Elizabeth

clean_body: We’re still fighting back. And we will win. | | | Happy Thanksgiving. This year has been one of incredible challenges and terrifying new realities. As I reflect today, I find so much to feel grateful for, even as we have been tested time and again. I’m grateful for every single person who has kept up the fight to build an America that reflects our best values. For the organizations and groups of volunteers that stepped up in the wake of the government shutdown to support communities. For the Americans who are taking a stand against the GOP’s unprecedented mid-decade gerrymandering attempts. For the people defending their neighbors who are being disappeared by ICE. For the voters across the country who cast their ballots for candidates who demonstrated we CAN make our economy more affordable and fairer for everyone. For the volunteers and staffers who supported this year’s campaigns — knocking on doors, making calls, talking with their families, taking a leap of faith, and pouring themselves into a movement they believed in. For the millions and millions of people across the country who showed up to declare loudly and clearly: We have No Kings in America. For the workers demanding better conditions for themselves and their colleagues. For my family, Bruce, and Bailey (and our walks around the pond). And I’m grateful for everyone on this team — you — and our shared belief in dreaming big, fighting hard, and making big, structural change together. We’re not accepting defeat. We’re still fighting back. And we will win. Wherever you are for the holiday, I hope the food is good and the company is warm. Remember, everyone should do their fair share: bring a dish, set the table, clean up — or, for any billionaires at your table, pay their fair share in taxes. Happy Thanksgiving — and thanks for being a part of this, Elizabeth | | | | | | | | | ---|---|---|--- | Paid for by Warren for Senate All content © 2025 Warren for Senate, All Rights Reserved PO Box 171375, Boston, MA 02117 | | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com We'll miss you, but you can always .

name: Scott Peters
subject: Wishing you a warm Thanksgiving
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scott Peters for Congress
body:
---
| | Peter,

I want to wish you and your family a warm and happy Thanksgiving. I hope you’re able to rest, enjoy time with loved ones, and take a moment to reflect on what matters most.

This year, I’m especially grateful for my family, my friends, and our entire CA-50 community – including you, Peter. It remains an honor to serve San Diego, and I’m thankful every day for the trust you place in me.

If you’re able, I hope you’ll join me in supporting neighbors who may be struggling this season. You can find your local food bank here: [**https://www.feedingamerica.org/find-your-local-foodbank**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120113514/585272366/1380771237?refcode2=4ba985bc-b6cb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&refcode=SP_EM_EN_180DO_251127_1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzkvMS84ODA2MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0YmE5ODViYy1iNmNiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=uyOIodO6U-cAsxVlzmyYF2IBObAqcXkE8nwHUdN3MXo=&emci=1988dc0a-08cb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=4ba985bc-b6cb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=5258741)

And here are a few major food banks serving San Diego communities:
• San Diego Food Bank – [**https://sandiegofoodbank.org**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120113517/585272369/-1719227438?refcode2=4ba985bc-b6cb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&refcode=SP_EM_EN_180DO_251127_1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzkvMS84ODA2MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0YmE5ODViYy1iNmNiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=uyOIodO6U-cAsxVlzmyYF2IBObAqcXkE8nwHUdN3MXo=&emci=1988dc0a-08cb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=4ba985bc-b6cb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=5258741)
• Feeding San Diego – [**https://feedingsandiego.org**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120113520/585272372/2111213044?refcode2=4ba985bc-b6cb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&refcode=SP_EM_EN_180DO_251127_1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzkvMS84ODA2MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0YmE5ODViYy1iNmNiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=uyOIodO6U-cAsxVlzmyYF2IBObAqcXkE8nwHUdN3MXo=&emci=1988dc0a-08cb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=4ba985bc-b6cb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=5258741)
• San Diego Rescue Mission – [**https://www.sdrescue.org**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120113523/585272375/1385649754?refcode2=4ba985bc-b6cb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&refcode=SP_EM_EN_180DO_251127_1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzkvMS84ODA2MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0YmE5ODViYy1iNmNiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=uyOIodO6U-cAsxVlzmyYF2IBObAqcXkE8nwHUdN3MXo=&emci=1988dc0a-08cb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=4ba985bc-b6cb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=5258741)

From my family to yours, I hope you have a safe, joyful, and restorative holiday.

With gratitude,

Scott

name: Katie Porter
subject: Happy Thanksgiving, Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
For me, Thanksgiving is a reminder of why I and so many other parents work so hard.
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=40490%2E3422442%2EoD3j4w)
Peter,
Happy Thanksgiving! I hope you are enjoying the day with friends, family, and loved ones.
I’m taking a day off from the campaign trail to spend the day with my dad. I am so thankful to be celebrating today with him after his 10 months of surgery, rehab, chemo, and radiation from bone cancer.
[](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20251127_1_ak.wZSyIY&refcode=em20251127-40490&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=40490%2E3422442%2EoD3j4w)
Today is a reminder to cherish our loved ones and the community members who show up for us in big and small ways — our caregivers, nurses, neighbors, and friends who help us get through the hardest days.
That’s why I’m running for governor: so every family can thrive, not just barely get by, and so no one has to choose between caring for the people they love and paying the bills.
From my family to yours, Happy Thanksgiving! I’m grateful for your support in this race.
–Katie

Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4g-em-footer?refcode=em20251127-40490&amounts=&t=4&refcode2=40490_3422442_oD3j4w&akid=40490%2E3422442%2EoD3j4w)**

* [Unsubscribe](https://act.katieporter.com/unsubscribe/kp4g-unsubscribe?source=40490&t=6&akid=40490%2E3422442%2EoD3j4w)

name: Stop ICE via Our Revolution
subject: Add your name: Defund ICE NOW!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ stdin# ]Our Revolution Logo

Peter Willis,

As families across the country gather for Thanksgiving, ICE is tearing
parents from their children and ripping families apart.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34713?source=email-20251127&t=1011&akid=9912%2E5885482%2E27qwB9 ]Headline: She was deported in error: her child was left behind.

One mother was working her factory shift when ICE agents stormed in. She
was separated from her children, detained, and mistakenly deported to
Guatemala – all before her 2-year-old son could even say goodbye.

This horror isn’t an isolated incident. Last week, a U.S. citizen high
school student was detained at lunch by immigration agents. No reason, no
warning. Just sheer state violence – unleashed on our friends and
neighbors.

This is Trump’s ICE: masked raids on neighborhoods, separating loved ones
using intimidation and brute force. These attacks are tearing apart
families, leaving children stranded, and putting entire communities under
siege. We cannot afford to stay silent.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34713?source=email-20251127&t=1012&akid=9912%2E5885482%2E27qwB9 ]This Thanksgiving, let’s send a clear message to Congress – we will NOT
stand by and allow Trump to rip our families and communities apart. Add
your name and tell Congress to stop funding ICE’s racist terror campaign,
NOW.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34713?source=email-20251127&t=1013&akid=9912%2E5885482%2E27qwB9 ]ADD YOUR NAME

ICE under Trump has morphed into a full-blown secret police force. Masked
officers using military hardware prowl neighborhoods, refuse to identify
themselves, and “disappear” people without warning or cause. This is not
safety. This is taxpayer-funded terror.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34713?source=email-20251127&t=1014&akid=9912%2E5885482%2E27qwB9 ]Headline: US immigration enforcement using military hardware and
tactics on civilians

Let’s be clear: ICE is targeting people based on their accents, the color
of their skin, and where they go to school or work. Millions of Americans
across the country are now afraid to leave their homes. This is a
humanitarian emergency on American soil.

But people are coming together to fight back. In Charlotte, over 30,000
students staged a walkout, protesting the federal invasion of their
communities. In cities and towns across the country, people are demanding
justice. Our Revolution’s Citizen Action Team is working to organize rapid
response protests to fight back – calling out corporations like AT&T and
Avelo Airlines that are collaborating with ICE.

The only reason ICE is able to storm schools, factories, and homes is
because Congress keeps giving them billions. But right now, our
representatives have a choice. They can either keep writing blank checks
for ICE to invade our neighborhoods – or they can stand up for justice and
family unity.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34713?source=email-20251127&t=1015&akid=9912%2E5885482%2E27qwB9 ]This Thanksgiving, let’s send a clear message: We refuse to sit still
as families are ripped apart. We demand an end to Trump’s cruel
deportation machine. Will you send an urgent email directly to Congress
now?

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34713?source=email-20251127&t=1016&akid=9912%2E5885482%2E27qwB9 ]ADD YOUR NAME

When we organize, we win,

Our Revolution

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/9?t=1001&akid=9912%2E5885482%2E27qwB9 ]Facebook | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/10?t=1002&akid=9912%2E5885482%2E27qwB9 ]Twitter | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/11?t=1003&akid=9912%2E5885482%2E27qwB9 ]Instagram | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/2355?t=1004&akid=9912%2E5885482%2E27qwB9 ]Youtube | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/2356?t=1005&akid=9912%2E5885482%2E27qwB9 ]Website |
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/10247?t=1006&akid=9912%2E5885482%2E27qwB9 ]Sign Up

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/26879?c0=email-20251127.dJY2Yc&t=1007&akid=9912%2E5885482%2E27qwB9 ]Our membership swag

Our Revolution is the largest entirely grassroots funded progressive
group in America.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/26880?c0=email-20251127.DoSoBz&t=1008&akid=9912%2E5885482%2E27qwB9 ]Help us defend democracy and take the fight directly to the
establishment by committing to be a Movement Builder with a recurring
donation of just $5 or more a month!

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/26881?c0=email-20251127.-ON4el&t=1009&akid=9912%2E5885482%2E27qwB9 ]GIVE MONTHLY

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/26882?c0=email-20251127.Yxpo_p&t=1010&akid=9912%2E5885482%2E27qwB9 ]GIVE ONCE

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.ourrevolution.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe-2/?akid=9912%2E5885482%2E27qwB9

Our Revolution’s Citizen Action Team is working to organize rapid response protests to fight back – calling out corporations like AT&T and Avelo Airlines that are collaborating with ICE. The only reason ICE is able to storm schools, factories, and homes is because Congress keeps giving them billions. But right now, our representatives have a choice. They can either keep writing blank checks for ICE to invade our neighborhoods – or they can stand up for justice and family unity. [ ]This Thanksgiving, let’s send a clear message: We refuse to sit still as families are ripped apart. We demand an end to Trump’s cruel deportation machine. Will you send an urgent email directly to Congress now? name: Sarah McBride
subject: Feeling grateful
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McBride for Delaware
body:
Taking a moment to say thank you for being part of our team.
#
Happy Thanksgiving! 🦃
Today, I’m thankful for family, friends, and the greatest state in the union, Delaware. I’m grateful every single day that Delawareans entrusted me with the responsibility of representing them in Congress. And I’m also deeply thankful for you.
A year ago this month, I was elected to Congress. I remember my first few days on Capitol Hill as a Representative-elect — getting lost in the tunnels (still happens!), rushing between orientation meetings, and wondering what the year had in store.
I think it’s safe to say my freshman year in Congress has been _anything_ but typical. But through every up and down, you’ve been right there with me.
Your kind messages, your choice to power this work, and your belief that real progress is always possible have meant more than you know. In tough moments, they’ve reminded me that we’re never alone — and why hope is worth holding onto.
So before I’m off to cook with my family — to some Taylor Swift, of course — I wanted to take a moment to say thank you for being part of our team. And thank you to the people of Delaware for giving me the honor of serving you.
With gratitude,
— Sarah McBride
* * *

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

name: Roy Cooper
subject: What I’m grateful for today (and asking you to help North Carolinians in need)
body:
Dear Peter,

I wanted to take just a moment out of your holiday to share my personal gratitude for your support over the last few months and for being with me through the months ahead. Campaigning isn’t always easy, but knowing that you’re with me makes the hard days worth it.

I hope you and your loved ones are enjoying this Thanksgiving and that you are safe, happy, and healthy this holiday season.

I know that for some North Carolinians, the holidays can be especially hard. If you’re in a position to help, I’d like to personally ask you to consider donating to the Food Bank of Central & Eastern North Carolina. Clicking this link or the one below will take you directly to the website to give. [https://secure.foodbankcenc.org/site/Donation]

DONATE TO FOOD BANK CENC [https://secure.foodbankcenc.org/site/Donation]

Happy Thanksgiving, and thank you.

Yours in gratitude,

Roy Cooper

Contact us [info@roycooper.com] |
Privacy Policy [https://roycooper.com/privacy-policy/]

Cooper Victory Fund
PO Box 1190
Raleigh, NC 27602

Paid for by Cooper Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Cooper for North Carolina, DSCC, and North Carolina Democratic Party — Federal.

Contributions or gifts to Cooper Victory Fund are not tax deductible.

The first $7,000 of each contribution from a person and the first $10,000 from a multicandidate committee (“PAC”) will be allocated to Cooper for North Carolina. The first $3,500 of a person’s contribution and the first $5,000 of a PAC’s contribution will be designated for the primary election; the next $3,500/$5,000 will be designated for the general election. The next $10,000 from a person and $5,000 from a PAC will be allocated to North Carolina Democratic Party – Federal. The next $44,300 of a contribution from a person and the next $15,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC General Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Headquarters Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Legal Fund. A contributor may designate his or her contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if following it would result in an excessive contribution.

Any donor history information in this email reflects what we have on file for this specific email address. If you have donated with a different email address, with a check, or with a spouse — thank you so much! We have that on file and cannot thank you enough for supporting this campaign.

By clicking the links above, you certify that you are responsible for paying all charges incurred in using the debit or credit card to be charged, and that your personal funds will be the true source of the contribution.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com . If you wish to no longer receive these messages, please unsubscribe. name: Greg Landsman
subject: What I’m grateful for this Thanksgiving
body:
| | |
---
| | , As I think about what I'm grateful for this year, Cincinnati and our community are at the top of the list.I'm grateful for the teachers who showed up every day for our kids, even when funding fell short. For the nurses and doctors who kept our community healthy. For the small business owners who took risks to keep their doors open. For the neighbors who looked out for each other when times got tough. As a dad of two teenagers, I'm especially grateful for the opportunity to raise my family in a community that still believes in looking out for one another.But gratitude isn't just about feeling thankful; it's about taking action. That's why I'm fighting every day in Congress to make sure hardworking families can afford groceries, keep the heat on this winter, and build a better future for their kids. This Thanksgiving, I hope you get to spend time with the people you love. You've earned it.And if you're one of the many families in Cincinnati volunteering at a food bank, serving meals, or helping neighbors in need, thank you. That's the spirit that makes our community special.Happy Thanksgiving!Appreciate you,_Greg_ 💙🙏
---
| | Landsman for Congress
PO Box 413
Cincinnati, OH 45201
United States
---
---

name: Jimmy Panetta
subject: Wishing you a meaningful Thanksgiving
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jimmy Panetta for Congress 2026, FEC ID# C00592154
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120113717/585273439/-2113420158?source=EM_NBI_JP_20251127_ST_Thanksgiving_0_6MD_Jimmypanetta_NA_NA_NA&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTkwMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZGY5N2I2MC1iOWNiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=A-0JPjljilkJC_zy0U4m2asUEfqY1NJPYWai9gtAphA=&emci=3f8d32bc-f5ca-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=2df97b60-b9cb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=6108787) | Peter,

As we gather with family, friends, and loved ones this Thanksgiving, I want to take a moment to share my gratitude for the people and communities that make California’s 19th District such a remarkable place.

Every day, I see the generosity, resilience, and fellowship that defines our community. From teachers supporting students through uncertain times, to health care workers and first responders keeping us safe, to families helping one another make ends meet – these acts of care remind me what public service is all about.

I’m deeply grateful for the trust you’ve placed in me to represent our 19th Congressional District. It’s an honor I carry with me every single day, and I remain committed to fighting for the values and future of the place that we proudly call home.

Wishing you a peaceful, healthy, and meaningful Thanksgiving.

— Jimmy Panetta
---

name: Ryan Housley
subject: They’re serving today. Let’s thank them.
body:
| | | [ ](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/byT9/?__vbtrk=MzM2MzEwOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzM2MzEwOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
---
| Dear Patriot, Today, as Americans gather around their tables and bow their heads in gratitude, thousands of our brave men and women in uniform are spending Thanksgiving **far from home** . No familiar voices. No warm meal with family. No break from duty. They’re out there...on ships, in the desert, on bases around the world...protecting everything we’re grateful for. That’s why HeroBox exists. And today, on Thanksgiving, we’re asking from the heart: [ **Will you take a moment to thank a hero who can’t be home today?** ](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/byT9/?__vbtrk=MzM2MzEwOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzM2MzEwOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct) Your gift sends a HeroBox filled with treats, comforts, and messages of love that remind a soldier, sailor, airman, or Marine that their sacrifice is seen... and deeply appreciated. If there is ever a day to step up for them, it’s today. [ **Will you send a HeroBox to a hero serving right now?** ](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/byT9/?__vbtrk=MzM2MzEwOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzM2MzEwOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct) **[Give a Thanksgiving Gift](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/byT9/?__vbtrk=MzM2MzEwOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzM2MzEwOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct) ** Even a small act of gratitude makes a world of difference to someone spending this day in uniform. From all of us at HeroBox:
Thank you for your patriotism. Thank you for your generosity.
And thank you for remembering the heroes who can’t be home today. Sincerely, Ryan Housley

clean_body: | | | | Dear Patriot, Today, as Americans gather around their tables and bow their heads in gratitude, thousands of our brave men and women in uniform are spending Thanksgiving far from home . No familiar voices. No warm meal with family. No break from duty. They’re out there...on ships, in the desert, on bases around the world...protecting everything we’re grateful for. That’s why HeroBox exists. And today, on Thanksgiving, we’re asking from the heart: Your gift sends a HeroBox filled with treats, comforts, and messages of love that remind a soldier, sailor, airman, or Marine that their sacrifice is seen... and deeply appreciated. If there is ever a day to step up for them, it’s today. Even a small act of gratitude makes a world of difference to someone spending this day in uniform. From all of us at HeroBox: Thank you for your patriotism. Thank you for your generosity. And thank you for remembering the heroes who can’t be home today. Sincerely, Ryan Housley | | HeroBox is a 501c3 Charitable Institution. All Contributions are Tax-Deductible. body:

Every step of the way, I have been grateful for the countless acts of kindness, inspiration, and resilience that our neighbors have shown.| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120114987/585278678/-1283252511?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDU0NTUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNzcxOTc4ZTAtYmJjYi1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=HTUMUUhk07qmUa5FMq7MtKIKPOVhAImoKpThDF1KjNg=&emci=7178410b-bbcb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=771978e0-bbcb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=12466492)

| | Friend —
---
This has been a challenging year for our communities.
---
Every step of the way, I have been grateful for the countless acts of kindness, inspiration, and resilience that our neighbors have shown.
---
It remains an honor and a privilege to serve you in Congress.
---
In this season of gratitude and giving, I hope that you are able to enjoy the holiday.
---
Happy Thanksgiving! Gabe
---

name: Charlene Fernandez, Chair
subject: A message of thanks
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE ARIZONA DEMOCRATIC PARTY
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/azd-em-header [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/azd-em-header]
From all of us at AZ Dems: Happy Thanksgiving!

As we gather with family and friends this week, I'm reminded of what makes Arizona special: our ability to come together across our differences and find common ground.

Growing up in rural Arizona, I learned that our communities are strongest when we listen to each other, respect our differences, and work together toward shared goals. That's never been more important than it is right now.

This Thanksgiving, I'm grateful for all of you. Your support, your commitment to Arizona, and your willingness to stay engaged even when things get tough — that's what gives me hope for our state's future.

Whether you're gathering around the table with people who share your views or with those who see things differently, I hope you'll keep the dialogue going. Real progress happens when we're willing to listen, to learn, and to find ways forward together.

It's going to take all of us to build a better Arizona — one that works for everyone, no matter where you live or what party you belong to.

Enjoy your Thanksgiving. I'm grateful to be in this fight with you.

Charlene Fernandez
Chair, Arizona Democratic Party
Arizona Democratic Party
PO Box 36123
Phoenix, AZ 85067-6123
United States
info@azdem.org [info@azdem.org]
PAID FOR BY THE ARIZONA DEMOCRATIC PARTY. NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE. GREG FREEMAN, TREASURER. WWW.AZDEM.ORG

name: Jason Crow
subject: Happy Thanksgiving
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jason Crow for Congress
body:
Jason Crow For Colorado [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/88523/images/Screenshot%202025-08-21%20at%2010.50.41%E2%80%AFAM.png]
Happy Thanksgiving, team.

Today, I want to take a moment to give thanks to you. Your dedication to improving our state is what helps me do important work in Congress.

Now more than ever, it’s important that we keep each other safe and ensure folks have the resources they need to get by.

It’s up to us to do everything we can to take care of our communities during the holiday season, so everyone has the opportunity to fully enjoy much needed time with the people they love.

Today, will you chip in any amount to support the Food Bank of the Rockies? [https://give.foodbankrockies.org/page/76289/donate/1?_gl=1*1v2gu5x*_gcl_au*NjkzNjA0NTkyLjE3NjM5OTM2ODA.&_ga=2.182012839.1483175530.1763993681-334244646.1763993681]

DONATE [https://give.foodbankrockies.org/page/76289/donate/1?_gl=1*1v2gu5x*_gcl_au*NjkzNjA0NTkyLjE3NjM5OTM2ODA.&_ga=2.182012839.1483175530.1763993681-334244646.1763993681 ]

Putting service above self is especially important during this holiday season. I hope you’ll consider joining me in supporting our local community.

Thanks for your help,

Jason
Jason Crow for Congress
8547 E Arapahoe Road, STE J-543
Greenwood Village, CO 80112
United States

name: Tammy Baldwin
subject: I’m hoping you can help me make a difference
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TAMMY BALDWIN FOR SENATE
body:
|
# [](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/67?t=1&akid=5125%2E35263%2EKe9LHc)
Hi there, Before you log off to spend time with loved ones for Thanksgiving, I’m hoping you can help me make a difference in the lives of those struggling to put food on the table this holiday season. Meals on Wheels operates in virtually every community in America to address senior hunger and isolation. Year after year, this team has stepped up to help provide food to the most vulnerable in our country. But now, food insecurity is on the rise as the Trump administration politicizes nutrition assistance. It’s more important than ever that we step up for our community. **I’m hoping this team can come together again and help provide Thanksgiving meals for folks who need it most this season.[So, if you’re able, will you consider joining me in supporting Meals on Wheels with a charitable donation of any amount before Thanksgiving?](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/4728?t=4&akid=5125%2E35263%2EKe9LHc)** | [DONATE](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/4728?t=5&akid=5125%2E35263%2EKe9LHc)
---
Thanks for everything. I hope you have a safe and healthy Thanksgiving holiday.
— Tammy
P.O. BOX 696 MADISON, WI 53701
**[www.tammybaldwin.com](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/67?t=2&akid=5125%2E35263%2EKe9LHc) | [INFO@TAMMYBALDWIN.COM](mailto:INFO@TAMMYBALDWIN.COM) | [Privacy Policy](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/68?t=3&akid=5125%2E35263%2EKe9LHc)**

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

subject: What Charlie knew all along

Charlie showed us how to WIN

Peter, Charlie Kirk knew something long before the rest of America caught on. That the next generation of young Americans were desperate for** ** truth... That the Left's grip on the culture wasn't as strong as it looked... And with boldness, hard work, and a focused strategy -- WE COULD WIN. Charlie didn't just talk about it. _He built a movement that's changed history._ **And long before he left us... he wrote _How We Win_**** -- the playbook that lays it all out.**

Peter, this isn't just a book... **It 's the exact blueprint Charlie used to:** 🔥 Launch Turning Point USA chapters in all 50 states
🔥 Wake up more than 820,000 student activists
🔥 Inspire a historic generational shift toward FREEDOM Charlie saw it coming before anyone else did. He showed the world that the American Culture War wasn't lost. While the media said Gen Z was gone, Charlie believed in them. He met them face to face. On the campuses. At the rallies. In the fight. He proved the Left and the Fake News Media wrong. **He showed us HOW WE WIN.**
---
[ ](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=258db8594ecf4a3aa846cdaf50e11e2b&_e=Ls0DctgXQpDVQ-ZTHUsqY8Nx9CyXCc0YbORK1FTjAGKwZpwxzi95ynUlJTv6EIdal6BA1bstOAoY09OejoQ01C22rHlwQHUHiAI58BvvMBs1eh0TfpDSUZru-6ivhRtOc8USWQPGbWwCRCdD2NxKMssdy2_B98hZoJCnVb_8gIC03_GClDVv6Ci7xivsOqA9OF4S7qIdXYpgqpxlmufk91eUEMm17yy62IW_xT9Vvo21ax4NSbKrnQYzlp5jPTSMCF2OUkRIiZz0C1RyZ4EnUHkkPjeDmA5c_UTK4NzxdlKLZfY3MOiWs0w_k5ZNoBKL-wLrPnF8tk1xlhNp8oWu4ITE1yMHPavR5L30YO5-1H8PIbfXNTz3Q0cyPIzjy1ErjtcNTh1q9sDLwW8P-vFLT2tuNnvpif23Pe9xSBMUIQTIGrRmvedMJCWvCUCOhF6DHJXfoUVQbaeO6jzbBpU91Yx9t681fU2E_A6CJZg3vJnF7wQBpIvqufvMnvHfmlsj0JwZwPJOlm8QWvqIEtGNyDhgT58F5u4ti9pcwgnRcHsOx9qgPATWObz9tHAxpfdgxfRUjteR7yCm8RYknjiLgkBq4VcHSxhtTa2_6wZhg9P1VtzcaMOZ_ou8Er19ijH4UVfUX1gTsHsM2Efi_f3rmtxeVHA_wuatX4f2WBgtN6UZjX0CkdEevO8w2XVsiznqmt5uJTJgrRL_CWrC2GCmpg%3D%3D)
---
Now it's up to us to finish what he started. You and I have the opportunity to carry his torch, reach the next student, change more hearts and minds, and save the future of America. _But we can't do it without grassroots patriots like you._ **_How We Win_**** is the game plan -- written in Charlie's words, forged in real fights, and ready to be handed to the next bold American who says "YES, I'll stand up."** Let that be you, Peter. This book isn't a political piece... It's not about playing games in the D.C. swamp... It's a blueprint for taking back our culture and rebuilding the foundations of this country. **Faith. Freedom. Family. Truth.** The Left is still attacking those values every single day. But now we have the roadmap to FIGHT BACK. Charlie didn't just write it -- he lived it. Every single day. [ **Make your gift to join Turning Point USA today, and we’ll send you your FREE copy of _How We Win_ today.** ](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=258db8594ecf4a3aa846cdaf50e11e2b&_e=Ls0DctgXQpDVQ-ZTHUsqY8Nx9CyXCc0YbORK1FTjAGKwZpwxzi95ynUlJTv6EIdal6BA1bstOAoY09OejoQ01C22rHlwQHUHiAI58BvvMBs1eh0TfpDSUZru-6ivhRtOc8USWQPGbWwCRCdD2NxKMssdy2_B98hZoJCnVb_8gIC03_GClDVv6Ci7xivsOqA9OF4S7qIdXYpgqpxlmufk91eUEMm17yy62IW_xT9Vvo21ax4NSbKrnQYzlp5jPTSM8vU3L_BndOXXggHcLGWpB4MzjFVHxXZBP5LtVzPpnr7jboxHiP3hGb3YqrJMHXLqxJbqN92t7pctrnToLd0k-wtgaDZQqSDyzCZm59emtGLldb5LPKP5j8EXUx8M5hj6tGyvPV1_O5rIPLYVrFiEukkfWqu5vaXwVovsi_Kxech6czLDE9cdbISsBdjFVCM85vzkF4D8JdHMkphPy1yiXUQ_vMgRPbFhTFkw-GMr5HMM4IKFpSI8ycQwyjroILYJ1OmV_eLvtUP8OuyU7jCi74mz8mkE6Ww7zUr2AOIma8AAjCFxUpiCC94kAat-ARuTEU10AWzFwvJc5YgpusQYoJ58aS77lyQBpK2HJKumd1x2TnA3_gLx4URo6qQped5XqY9oAMlFgClZA1fV-aIpcPZVPWf9jcVF-AHl-3rZpMO6KSGy1VIqNnZDtDL-Wz0U6d7zfAkMpfV8wyvnDRTZSw%3D%3D)

name: Phil Weiser
subject: Warm Thanksgiving wishes, from me to you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Phil Weiser for Colorado
body:
Fear is contagious, but so are courage and hope

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120115494/585280426/1239926708?refcode=em_WEI_OTH_Gratitude_112725&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiNjAzNDUxZS1iZmNiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2e7j4C7gdDdbdsmHjOA_SSKkvn6MWHI2YRiWxLCBqAw=&emci=fcbb7e85-e6ca-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=b603451e-bfcb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=51844520) Hi folks, it’s Phil. As we celebrate Thanksgiving today, I am thinking about the sources of gratitude, inspiration and hope that keep us going when times are tough. These are just a few of mine: **My wonderful family** — my wife and our two children, as well as my grandparents and mother, who survived a Nazi concentration camp. Although my grandparents are no longer with us, my memories of their resilience and love for our nation’s principles live on as a blessing. Over the years, my grandmother’s stories of survival and reasons to never give up have stayed with me — including this piece of wisdom: as long as you hold onto hope, nothing is lost. “It is easier to believe,” she’d say. I continue to hold that spirit in my heart. **_Every single conversation you and I have had_ **over the course of this campaign. I am humbled by your support and deeply appreciate your engagement. **The leadership of Colorado governors who’ve come before us** — like Billy Adams, who took on the Klan and won. Like Ralph Carr, who refused to put Japanese-Americans in internment camps even when ordered to by the federal government. And like Roy Romer, who stood strong against discriminatory policies and protected LGBTQ+ equality long before others did so. **The rule of law** , our commitment to freedom and opportunity for all, and our 250-year-old (and counting!) democratic republic. | | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120115495/585280427/109727453?refcode=em_WEI_OTH_Gratitude_112725&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiNjAzNDUxZS1iZmNiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2e7j4C7gdDdbdsmHjOA_SSKkvn6MWHI2YRiWxLCBqAw=)
---
There is so much to be grateful for this Thanksgiving. From my family to yours, I wish you all the happiest of Thanksgiving and an opportunity to find hope, joy, and support with friends and family during the holiday.
\- Phil
|
---
Make sure this email goes to your inbox. Add teamphil@philforcolorado.com to your address book.

Peter, I wish you a happy and safe Thanksgiving. I hope you are surrounded today by loved ones and have many things to be grateful for.

# [#]I am grateful to serve the people in our district, for the chance to keep fighting for you and addressing the significant challenges we face. Thank you for your ongoing support, it is greatly appreciated.

Have a happy and safe holiday.

With gratitude,

Mike

Gratitude is the gateway to joy

| | | | | [](https://eot.davidpanforcongress.com/f/a/GpzMVCKbDKwtyvdY8cYukg~~/AAAHURA~/qDmTDelK3N8--g_9Y1om8grbKra35O9Q5dARLJUnDkgk4won_DSfxI3DLYu_5x3e_uJocUy3A87qBHjLqBaSo_r6v7O--AKZ6MKDZFXD4e05MIrcJyEpm3ExLLAla43H8Fop4dCPwJTeiObHHCiQ4r0Rfm8d9gbpuKzFln6GPqI~)
---
| | At the Placentia Prayer Breakfast last week, Pastor Jay Hewitt emphasized that gratitude is the gateway to joy. His completion of an Ironman Triathlon while fighting cancer taught us how his own gratitude, even in the most difficult of circumstances, could lead to joy for himself and those around him. His actions and words were a true inspiration, and I was grateful to be in the audience. This Thanksgiving, I am grateful to all the friends and supporters who have moved us closer to the goal of bringing freedom and opportunity to our nation’s families and communities. I wish you all a happy Thanksgiving!
Candidate for Congress in California’s 46th district [DavidPanforCongress.com](https://eot.davidpanforcongress.com/f/a/XXKxBU85JKJ3H60iLA2n9Q~~/AAAHURA~/BnU75FdFSQ5qtnuOUBiDaoP2UATN1-s8naJDIGme6TUW5ttZaEhREJ7wEHRbHnHQaaLNBLhRamUIOr3bzoColU7pQYFIERY6F-zCrGtTjGkhfJqmbsSvsdBsGSkxaz8MsK-bg19zm6qFr2BOSxFSwWqyCSi_Cer6gZFhK_Y5cJ4~)

Dear USO Supporter,
This Thanksgiving, many of our brave service members are thousands of miles away from home. While you're gathering with family or dear friends, they're alone in a remote location. You may be laughing, cheering on your favorite football team and creating memories while they're spending another day in a lonely barracks.
**This is the hardest time of year for our heroes deployed around the world.** And this year, it's even harder for many. Loneliness, isolation and a longing for home can feel even more overwhelming.
That's why we're grateful to be a trusted partner in the NFL's Salute to Service initiative during a time when service members need our gratitude and support. To fill the gap of what we can currently provide, we've set a goal to fund at least 7,500 meals for our military heroes by Thanksgiving.
**[During this critical time of year for our service members, the NFL is generously matching your gift, dollar for dollar, up to a total of $50,000, to help us reach our goal of delivering warm meals and more to our brave heroes. Will you help us reach our Thanksgiving meal goal now?](https://links.e.uso.org/els/v2/LaXVHgwdy-Tq/ZjVhVTJkc0I1bHhxUTVibXNLYTBRQlh2WDF1RHF0NW00ZFJxcTNqUjBqeTR3Ym5INWJIL2duQm9lRWozRWtGRDZmbDFBcEV3MGtjSHpVbFpGUGV6b05CTjFScy84bm9jc25SM3JLdWVxczg9S0/ZGxyNFlZMHN4RE1Jd3JRSXVZdWVBV1ZtZks5bW5KUFBXMExUM1FmcU5lWEVMa1dBQkVhRUU0OUdkU3pWUFJxNTZ0TFZmNzljL1RxYTFTWVVYZXFBQ292S3NydlRqaEc1S0)**

**For our troops deployed to the farthest reaches of the world, a warm meal goes a long way. It 's not just a meal -- it's a source of comfort, a boost in morale and a reminder that they're not forgotten during the holiday season. **
**[Will you help us reach our Thanksgiving meal goal now?](https://links.e.uso.)**

On behalf of our troops,
The USO

Your contribution will be joined with the contributions of others and used where it is needed most to strengthen the well-being of the people serving in America's military and their families.

name: Marie Gluesenkamp Perez
subject: Happy Thanksgiving!
body:
| | Peter – From my family to yours, Happy Thanksgiving!

We have a lot to be thankful for this year. I’m especially grateful for the ongoing support from folks like you.

Whether you’ve been here since the beginning, last year, or last week – I know how lucky I am to have you on our team, and I can’t thank you enough.

It’s an honor to serve WA-03 in Congress for another term, and I’m looking forward to everything we will accomplish together on the road ahead.

Let’s all take this opportunity to reflect and give thanks – and eat lots of great food, of course.

Peter, I hope you and your family have a safe and happy holiday.

– Marie

---
|
* * *
---
| | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120115682/585281250/-1644157728?amount=25&refcode=MGP_EM_EN_180DO_251127_1_1&refcode2=88adbd36-c1cb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTkyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4OGFkYmQzNi1jMWNiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=l6gJ-SRF6Poabg0A1-nU44J2LPm-6_OxxK3kyS35CiE=)

clean_body: | | Peter – From my family to yours, Happy Thanksgiving! We have a lot to be thankful for this year. I’m especially grateful for the ongoing support from folks like you. Whether you’ve been here since the beginning, last year, or last week – I know how lucky I am to have you on our team, and I can’t thank you enough. It’s an honor to serve WA-03 in Congress for another term, and I’m looking forward to everything we will accomplish together on the road ahead. Let’s all take this opportunity to reflect and give thanks – and eat lots of great food, of course. Peter, I hope you and your family have a safe and happy holiday. – Marie | | | = | Marie Gluesenkamp Perez is a fifth-generation Washingtonian, a small business owner and a pro-choice mother. name: Marialana
subject: A moment of Thanks & a DC recap
body:
Leading the march, meeting heroes, and growing our coalition.

| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://kinterforcongress.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f6a723b5c3bc1add1f7a8ca4d&id=8c9a0759ae&e=d995135712)
| |
#### **Holiday Update!**
---
| |
## Leading the Charge in D.C.
---
| |

As we head into the holiday, I am filled with immense gratitude for this community. Because of your support, I was able to travel to Washington D.C. last week to represent Central Florida at the "Remove the Regime" mobilization.

**It was a surreal and inspiring experience. I had the honor of speaking at the National Veterans Rally** and then helping **lead a march of tens of thousands of Americans** from the rally to the Capitol steps.
The energy was undeniable, and the message was clear: we will not tolerate the erosion of our democracy.

---
[|
---](https://kinterforcongress.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f6a723b5c3bc1add1f7a8ca4d&id=2557204156&e=d995135712)
[|
---](https://kinterforcongress.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f6a723b5c3bc1add1f7a8ca4d&id=89f97b88a5&e=d995135712)
| |
### **New Endorsements!**
---
| |

While there, I connected with incredible leaders who are fighting for truth and justice, and I am thrilled to announce that our momentum has earned us **two major new endorsements:**
* **Michael Fanone:** A former D.C. Metropolitan Police officer who was nearly killed defending the Capitol on January 6th. A _New York Times_ bestselling author and recipient of the Presidential Citizens Medal, Michael continues to speak out against political violence and fight for the soul of America.

* **Lt. Col. (Ret.) Anthony Aguilar:** A retired Special Forces Green Beret who served for 25 years. He recently became a whistleblower, courageously exposing misconduct at aid distribution sites in Gaza based on his commitment to American values.

---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
|
---
| |
---
| |
### **THANK YOU!**
---
| |

This movement is growing every single day, and it is all because of you. This is the proof that a campaign funded by the people, for the people, can and will win.
---
| |
Thank you for being in this fight with me.
**In Solidarity,****Marialana Kinter**

---
|
---
| |

Everything you've just read is a direct result of our grassroots supporters. If you're inspired by what we've built, please consider making a contribution today to help us keep this movement growing.

---

| | |

name: Steve Daines
subject: Happy Thanksgiving
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Steve Daines for Montana
body:
I am thankful for your dedication, support, and belief in our shared values. |
---
Fellow American,

From my family to yours, I want to wish you a very happy Thanksgiving. On this day of Thanksgiving, we celebrate and give thanks to God for our many blessings. We can be truly thankful for the great country we are blessed to live in, and for our freedom and the hard-working men and women who make it possible.

Like our men and women in the armed forces who are serving far away from home today, protecting the ideals our nation holds dear.

This season reminds us of the blessings we enjoy together. May your Thanksgiving be filled with joy, love, and cherished moments with family and friends.

Thanks,

[](http://links.action.supportstevedaines.com/u/click?_t=71faa67ca88f4c4b899697b6a4621901&_m=a0b6551e415f41a9aaa7063ced088772&_e=J652ZpcmdZZHaGI0eXmDnJg_uMHmDXTHaIDaBJrWQknufONmS35OUkrsI-xHcSqarGxyRl4CHRhcW4OG8f9UJ2-ZR9t-F7X-yxb89LMu-KpyWNLjzPNSIAewSyO1ezXu6ocjAFQKT4LgCxxZQ2SHch6D5wYvxJGwaUJQ_6epj8FIkAcjyWqk6H2cYTI8LR2Da0wzsmmeIU-M9hbrvoACQSDx58eKlXhN1VfU6qnQ6r4gJL6K83I8ixqIN6F9xYBkkJPBIqmsa047hi-E7lHpTG3VlI3EBFjLplyZkKYBNryXdCQvXepCiJLQCrKpfTu7)

name: Rose Penelope Yee
subject: With Thanksgiving Gratitude — and a Look Ahead
disclaimer_text: Paid for by [Rose for Congress](https://roseforcongress
body:
Honored by your past support—and ready to take the next step for peace and justice.

| | | | | [|
---](https://roseforcongress.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=35bf91c695e8f4a5dc8a9dd42&id=68a8e4288a&e=8c53948e4c)
| | | | [|
---](https://roseforcongress.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=35bf91c695e8f4a5dc8a9dd42&id=f3ddfeb5b7&e=8c53948e4c)
| | With Gratitude — and a Look Ahead
---
| |

Dear friends,

As we gather with loved ones this Thanksgiving, I want to extend my heartfelt gratitude for your trust, support, and partnership in our movement. In a year marked by economic turbulence—from the disruptions caused by Trump’s tariffs to Medicaid cuts in the “One Big Beautiful Bill,” and the diversion of taxpayer dollars toward foreign wars and unstable regimes—these moments of connection and community feel more sacred than ever.

I also want to share something important: I am seriously considering a primary challenge to Rep. Jared Huffman. His vote for the 2024 Israel Supplemental Appropriations Act—effectively endorsing continued U.S. support for the genocide in Gaza—demands a moral response. Our district deserves a representative who will stand firmly for peace, human rights, and true progressive values.

If I move forward, our campaign will champion the priorities that have always anchored our movement: • Medicare for All
• Stop arming genocide; invest in peace
• A federal living wage
• Housing as a human right
• A Green New Deal–aligned climate agenda
• Repeal Citizens United and remove corporate money from politics

Thank you again for your support and for believing in a politics rooted in peace and justice. I’m excited for what we can build together—and I hope you’ll stand with me in the months ahead.

With deep gratitude and resolve,
**Rose Penelope Yee**

clean_body: Honored by your past support—and ready to take the next step for peace and justice. | | | | | | | | | | | With Gratitude — and a Look Ahead | | Dear friends, As we gather with loved ones this Thanksgiving, I want to extend my heartfelt gratitude for your trust, support, and partnership in our movement. In a year marked by economic turbulence—from the disruptions caused by Trump’s tariffs to Medicaid cuts in the “One Big Beautiful Bill,” and the diversion of taxpayer dollars toward foreign wars and unstable regimes—these moments of connection and community feel more sacred than ever. I also want to share something important: I am seriously considering a primary challenge to Rep. Jared Huffman. His vote for the 2024 Israel Supplemental Appropriations Act—effectively endorsing continued U.S. support for the genocide in Gaza—demands a moral response. Our district deserves a representative who will stand firmly for peace, human rights, and true progressive values. If I move forward, our campaign will champion the priorities that have always anchored our movement: • Medicare for All • Stop arming genocide; invest in peace • A federal living wage • Housing as a human right • A Green New Deal–aligned climate agenda • Repeal Citizens United and remove corporate money from politics Thank you again for your support and for believing in a politics rooted in peace and justice. I’m excited for what we can build together—and I hope you’ll stand with me in the months ahead. With deep gratitude and resolve, Rose Penelope Yee | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Paid for by Rose for Congress 3335 Placer St # 288 Redding, CA 96001-2364 * USA or |

Dear Peter,

All of us at the Center for Reproductive Rights want to take a moment to express our heartfelt gratitude for **your** generosity and unwavering commitment to protecting abortion rights.

Despite the constant attacks, barriers, and challenges, there is _so_ much to be thankful for—and because of you, we have hope for the future of reproductive freedom. [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/120116483/585284549/2141984067?sourceid=1150983&utm_campaign=26emkge-1112aax-on&utm_medium=ea&utm_source=email&utm_content=thanksgiving&amtOpts=&recurringAmtOpts=&am=&recurringAm=&is_optimized_ask=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9DRlJSL0NGUlIvMS85NTk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MDQzYzY0My1jNWNiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fIFoTt1KPmhB3DVrRxnex0X7vAQ2ljp0_YtAQpdgqpQ=&emci=01d09cfe-8ec5-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=8043c643-c5cb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=954237) Thank you for everything you do to make the fight for reproductive freedom a reality.

Warmly,

Nancy Northup

# [A Thanksgiving Update: We Keep Moving Forward](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=180129487&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgwMTI5NDg3LCJpYXQiOjE3NjQyNzMyNTgsImV4cCI6MTc2Njg2NTI1OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMyNjAxMTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.EAbkZCAphqFn5KzlP0D9MpAlfo99xprbkiLL9B4BLFk)
### A procedural delay means Zahid isn't home with us for the holiday; but we are so close to his freedom we can taste it.
| | [Melissa Chaudhry](https://substack.com/@melissa4congress)
---
| Nov 27
---
| [](https://substack.com/@melissa4congress)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=180129487&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgwMTI5NDg3LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2NDI3MzI1OCwiZXhwIjoxNzY2ODY1MjU4LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMzI2MDExMSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.K8LLlAKUr41K_RQUF_GusSCwiTx_qEL-dNjszsXGc3w&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/melissa4congress/p/a-thanksgiving-update-we-keep-moving?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Friends,

Yesterday was a full day -- of movement, of effort, of steady determination on behalf of my husband, and of gratitude for all of you who continue to walk with us. As the holiday begins and the federal courts close their doors for the long weekend, I want to share where things stand.

On Tuesday, the judge issued an order requiring us to correct a procedural detail in Zahid's case. It wasn't a setback -- if anything, it was another sign that the Court is paying close attention, giving us every possible opportunity to succeed, and keeping the path open for emergency relief. The judge has been remarkably patient, responsive, and humane. For that alone, I am deeply thankful.

On Wednesday, we filed everything the Court asked for:
• the Amended Habeas Petition naming the correct respondent (the NWIPC Warden, Bruce Scott),
• the renewed Temporary Restraining Order request,
• the updated incorporations of all evidence and declarations,
• and the full set of supporting documents already in the record.

In other words: the Court now has everything it needs to act.

After filing, I drove straight to the Northwest ICE Processing Center to personally serve Warden Scott, as required. I arrived at 3:55pm.

After a long wait, Lieutenant Stokes came out to speak with me. He checked to see if the Warden was in.
At 4:10pm, he returned and told me:
• the Warden had "left for the day,"
• no one present was authorized to receive service on his behalf,
• he would not accept service,
• he would not sign the receipt-of-service page I brought, and
• he would not provide written confirmation of our interaction.

So, I am documenting it here -- in real time, with photos and timestamp metadata -- in case it becomes necessary to show that I attempted service, in good faith, today, on November 26, 2025.

I will return Friday, during their stated business hours before 3pm, to attempt service again.

The federal courts are closed today and Friday for Thanksgiving, so the earliest the Court could take any further action on Zahid's case is Monday, December 1.

And Monday, as it happens, is our son Adil's first birthday.

We would like nothing more than to give him the gift of his father's freedom.

The thought that the judge could rule as early as Monday -- that Zahid could be home for our baby's first birthday -- is almost more than I can hold. But it is real. There is genuine forward motion in this case. Every filing, every deadline, every order is getting us closer to the moment we have fought so hard for.

And even in the middle of this ache -- this long season of waiting, hoping, fighting, and navigating a system that was not designed to show mercy -- I am thankful.

Thankful for the judge who has treated this case with seriousness and care.

Thankful for the attorneys, advocates, community members, and friends who have lifted us up.

Thankful for every single one of you who has prayed for us, checked in on us, held space for us, supported us financially, and believed alongside us.

Thankful that my children know, without question, that their father is a good man -- and that thousands of people agree.

Thankful that justice is moving, finally. Even if slowly. Even if imperfectly. It is moving.

Tonight, we are setting the table for Thanksgiving dinner with one place left open -- the place where Zahid belongs. The place that will be filled again. Maybe soon. Maybe very soon. I feel that in my bones.

Thank you for being part of our family's circle of strength.

May your Thanksgiving be warm, safe, and filled with the love of the people who hold you steady.
And may this be the last holiday we spend without Zahid at our table.

With gratitude,
Melissa
