clean_body: A procedural delay means Zahid isn't home with us for the holiday; but we are so close to his freedom we can taste it. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? A procedural delay means Zahid isn't home with us for the holiday; but we are so close to his freedom we can taste it. | | | Nov 27 | | | | | | | | | | | | Friends, Yesterday was a full day -- of movement, of effort, of steady determination on behalf of my husband, and of gratitude for all of you who continue to walk with us. As the holiday begins and the federal courts close their doors for the long weekend, I want to share where things stand. On Tuesday, the judge issued an order requiring us to correct a procedural detail in Zahid's case. It wasn't a setback -- if anything, it was another sign that the Court is paying close attention, giving us every possible opportunity to succeed, and keeping the path open for emergency relief. The judge has been remarkably patient, responsive, and humane. For that alone, I am deeply thankful. On Wednesday, we filed everything the Court asked for: • the Amended Habeas Petition naming the correct respondent (the NWIPC Warden, Bruce Scott), • the renewed Temporary Restraining Order request, • the updated incorporations of all evidence and declarations, • and the full set of supporting documents already in the record. In other words: the Court now has everything it needs to act. After filing, I drove straight to the Northwest ICE Processing Center to personally serve Warden Scott, as required. I arrived at 3:55pm. After a long wait, Lieutenant Stokes came out to speak with me. He checked to see if the Warden was in. At 4:10pm, he returned and told me: • the Warden had "left for the day," • no one present was authorized to receive service on his behalf, • he would not accept service, • he would not sign the receipt-of-service page I brought, and • he would not provide written confirmation of our interaction. So, I am documenting it here -- in real time, with photos and timestamp metadata -- in case it becomes necessary to show that I attempted service, in good faith, today, on November 26, 2025. I will return Friday, during their stated business hours before 3pm, to attempt service again. The federal courts are closed today and Friday for Thanksgiving, so the earliest the Court could take any further action on Zahid's case is Monday, December 1. And Monday, as it happens, is our son Adil's first birthday. We would like nothing more than to give him the gift of his father's freedom. The thought that the judge could rule as early as Monday -- that Zahid could be home for our baby's first birthday -- is almost more than I can hold. But it is real. There is genuine forward motion in this case. Every filing, every deadline, every order is getting us closer to the moment we have fought so hard for. And even in the middle of this ache -- this long season of waiting, hoping, fighting, and navigating a system that was not designed to show mercy -- I am thankful. Thankful for the judge who has treated this case with seriousness and care. Thankful for the attorneys, advocates, community members, and friends who have lifted us up. Thankful for every single one of you who has prayed for us, checked in on us, held space for us, supported us financially, and believed alongside us. Thankful that my children know, without question, that their father is a good man -- and that thousands of people agree. Thankful that justice is moving, finally. Even if slowly. Even if imperfectly. It is moving. Tonight, we are setting the table for Thanksgiving dinner with one place left open -- the place where Zahid belongs. The place that will be filled again. Maybe soon. Maybe very soon. I feel that in my bones. Thank you for being part of our family's circle of strength. May your Thanksgiving be warm, safe, and filled with the love of the people who hold you steady. And may this be the last holiday we spend without Zahid at our table. With gratitude, Melissa | | ---|---|--- You 're currently a free subscriber to "It's a Matter of Integrity" by Melissa Chaudhry. To support the mission and access the full experience, upgrade your subscription. | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Melissa Chaudhry 6727 Martin Luther King Jr Way S, Suite M, #1117 Seattle, WA 98118 ? ?

name: Nancy
subject: Happy Thanksgiving from the Dahlstroms!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nancy Dahlstrom For Governor P
body:
| | | Dear Peter, Before we gather around our table to share what we are grateful for this year, I want to send a quick note to let you know how thankful I am for your support and well-wishes this holiday. I'm thankful for the amazing family Kit and I share, including our four wonderful children and 11 grandchildren. Like so many Alaskan families, ours is touched by uniformed public service as our son serves with the Arizona National Guard, and our thoughts and appreciation are also with them this holiday. Speaking of my husband Kit, I cannot wait to eat his candied sweet potatoes, a Dahlstrom family tradition! **From the Dahlstrom family table to you and yours, Happy Thanksgiving!** Sincerely,
---
---
**Nancy Dahlstrom** _Alaska's Lt. Governor and 2026 Republican candidate for governor_
---
| |

clean_body: If we keep this up, she doesn’t stand a chance... | Friend, The outpouring of grassroots support I’ve received since officially announcing my gubernatorial campaign has been truly incredible. Within just a few hours of my announcement, we raised over $1 MILLION for my gubernatorial campaign and the New York GOP! Those kinds of numbers will send a shiver down Kathy Hochul’s spine. The worst governor in America knows she can’t win a free and fair election that’s decided on the issues. Her only hope is to BUY her way to reelection – so if we can keep this momentum going, she won’t stand a chance! But that last part is key, Friend... To compete with the Democrats’ multimillion-dollar ad buys and win this race, we have to keep our momentum going from today all the way through Election Day. That’s why I set an ambitious goal of raising an additional $1,000,000 by midnight tomorrow to ensure we close out our launch week in the strongest possible position. Let’s CRUSH this goal and show Hochul that her days in office are numbered! Thank you, Elise Stefanik Paid for by Elise for Governor PO Box 500 Glens Falls, NY 12801

subject: Peter, you blew me out of the water
body:

name: Jim MacEachern
subject: Please help me break the silence…
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the New Hampshire Republican State Committee, Chairman Jim MacEachern
body:
My cousin from Lowell said the unforgivable words...

| | | | | [|
---](https://gop.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6abc8216f2c93cdbede56dda3&id=fd93d1151a&e=f3aa6419cd)
| | | | | |

Friend,It’s happened.The dinner table is dead silent. Forks down. Eyes wide. You could hear a pin drop.Someone brought up politics again.And then — God help us — my cousin from Lowell said the unforgivable words:**“Honestly, Massachusetts is better than New Hampshire.”** _Grandma gasped.__A toddler started crying.__Even the turkey looked offended._ I tried to keep the peace, but I could feel every Granite Stater instinct in my bones telling me to stand up and deliver the Gettysburg Address of Live Free or Die.**But instead... I’m emailing you.****Because there’s only one way to break this tension and restore order to Thanksgiving:**[**Prove him wrong by helping the NHGOP fight back.**](https://gop.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6abc8216f2c93cdbede56dda3&id=adfcc4fbdb&e=f3aa6419cd) If we hit our Thanksgiving fundraising goal, I’ll have the perfect comeback — cold, hard proof that people are stepping up to defend low taxes, safe communities, and the New Hampshire way of life.Please. For the honor of our state. For the peace of this dinner table. For the love of everything not taxed at Massachusetts rates:[Chip in HERE right now:](https://gop.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6abc8216f2c93cdbede56dda3&id=f74706949b&e=f3aa6419cd)
---
| [SHOW WE’RE STRONGER](https://gop.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6abc8216f2c93cdbede56dda3&id=3d69f2f48f&e=f3aa6419cd)
---
| [DEFEND LIVE FREE OR DIE](https://gop.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6abc8216f2c93cdbede56dda3&id=a6a3908649&e=f3aa6419cd)
---
| [PUT HIM IN HIS PLACE](https://gop.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6abc8216f2c93cdbede56dda3&id=70d01c90bd&e=f3aa6419cd)
---
| |

Thank you — truly — and Happy Thanksgiving.Now excuse me while I try to explain to my cousin why we don’t need an income tax.— Chairman Jim MacEachern
---
| | | | | | | | | [View email in browser](https://mailchi.mp/nhgop/thanksgiving-silence?e=f3aa6419cd)
Paid for by the New Hampshire Republican State Committee, Chairman Jim MacEachern. Not Authorized by any Candidate or Candidate Committee
New Hampshire Republican Party * 10 Water St * Concord, NH 03301-4844 * USA

name: =?utf-8?q?Missing_Signature_=E2=9D=97=5Bprotectvoting=2Eorg=5D?=
subject: FWD: Thank Justice Sotomayor!!!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
You’re about to lose your signature spot...
| Trump’s Supreme Court majority is on the verge of DESTROYING the Voting Rights Act — they just agreed to hear the case!

**Sonia Sotomayor is one of our ONLY fighters for democracy left on the Court, but you still haven’t signed our letter thanking her for her work.** [**We’re giving you ONE more chance to sign by 8am tomorrow, or you will lose your signature spot. THANK JUSTICE SOTOMAYOR NOW »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/97121?t=6&akid=22020%2E878179%2EMb9MLv) | [**Donate $3 to sign the letter now »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/97121?t=7&akid=22020%2E878179%2EMb9MLv)
---
Thank you,

_The Voter Protection Project_
**–BEGIN FORWARDED MESSAGE–**
_Justice Sotomayor just put the MAGA SCOTUS majority & Donald Trump to SHAME:_
[](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/97121?t=8&akid=22020%2E878179%2EMb9MLv)
**If a member of the Supreme Court — _who is supposed to be impartial_ — is sounding the alarm, _we should ALL be listening._**

Team, we’re so grateful for Sotomayor’s BRAVE leadership — but Republicans are FURIOUS with Sotomayor, and they’re _plotting revenge._

We need to show Supreme Court Justice Sotomayor that Democrats (especially Democrats in your state) have her back!

[**As one of the FEW protectors of our democracy left on the Supreme Court, we’re sending Sotomayor a letter thanking her for her incredible work. WE HAVE ONLY 18 SPOTS LEFT:**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/97121?t=9&akid=22020%2E878179%2EMb9MLv)
[**Donate $3 to sign the letter now »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/97121?t=10&akid=22020%2E878179%2EMb9MLv)
---
TO: Justice Sonia Sotomayor
FROM: A proud Democrat
Subject: Thank you for taking a stand. **Dear Justice Sotomayor,

Thank you for standing up for our democracy against Donald Trump’s authoritarian agenda and blatant disregard of the Constitution.**

Your bravery continues to blow us away, time after time.

**As Trump tries to seize more and more control from Congress and the courts, your resilience is needed now more than ever.**

I’m sick and tired of the GOP letting him off scot-free — so I’m doing everything in my power to elect Democrats and ensure we appoint more amazing Supreme Court Justices like YOU in the near future.

[**I know you’re facing relentless attacks from Trump and his MAGA minions, so I’m signing this thank you letter and chipping in $3 to the Voter Protection Project to help Democrats keep fighting for justice »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/97121?t=11&akid=22020%2E878179%2EMb9MLv) [](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/97121?t=12&akid=22020%2E878179%2EMb9MLv) | [**Donate $3 to sign now »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/97121?t=13&akid=22020%2E878179%2EMb9MLv)
---
**_X A grateful Democrat_**
—The Voter Protection Project
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=22020%2E878179%2EMb9MLv). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=22020%2E878179%2EMb9MLv).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=22020%2E878179%2EMb9MLv) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=14&akid=22020%2E878179%2EMb9MLv). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=22020%2E878179%2EMb9MLv)
---

name: Tim Walz
subject: Wishing you a happy Thanksgiving 🦃
disclaimer_text: paid for by Walz for Governor
body:
I wanted to wish you and your loved ones a very happy Thanksgiving.
#
Hey, it’s Tim.
There’s a long list of things I’m thankful for this year.
I’m thankful for my wife Gwen and for our kids Hope and Gus.
I’m thankful for the hundreds of volunteers who’ve shown up for me again and again, and for the thousands of generous supporters who’ve pitched in to build up our campaign. Thank you for believing in all that we can accomplish together.
Most of all, I’m just unbelievably grateful you’re on this team, Peter. It means the world to me. So I hope you have a day filled with lots of turkey, traditions (new and old), and quality time with your loved ones.
— Tim

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

One participant—blindfolded and hands free—set out to trial Tesla’s newest AI feature.

No remote. No driver. No contingency.

The team watched and recorded every moment.

Then the truck engaged—driven solely by AI.

Each second was authentic, every movement captured.

Engineers and industry observers have been taken aback by the results.

It wasn’t a trick—it was a direct look at the leading edge of automotive autonomy.

Early attention is building around this breakthrough.

[Watch the video that’s prompting conversation across the tech world.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=944f44a3b1554f70b0529ffdc281ecd0&_e=RRvw6ElqWEZyoXgdOTfQUrdvQNMli14KTLyTrfwRiGrEb-bW3Z8Tf019SnBc0YeTEzzw_aksp1oTOLWKor-J1c5Dvy1wh-g-FRaClzRJN-flSWYyn5BzWtWFX37LlbpgzdEDYrHhWrYdjojRpM7bTdeEiHH5UBrAeOld40HnxLmk9tn6Yjh4XposRxytHcSl4_dnJ4v8MD6bc0Fu8xh7L0Ixu1SLoaMrVhzWKSA9uteU0bdvSMc08mt8q6HoaJKNbfN_ORZ0aL22EAdVvqVpfZdcqKsl3nSwsPVqLVDcCltIa7Iu7HiyJVTo33SvTItL2ciIQ2JcxFgCVXGkrWQ074NSNtBnDdtAkAkp38NE0RQCSJTwIio9pvhjzYQruNfiXB8FcwoxaSPlb4IXMIcyQzacdTsD02FXmjHByobMf16k_V58N-5HI1HzqWbSDW3Nzh7JuJgIHnFGlZA7Yiim3NK3ifeQbQu6jlTWk5Us1pWKMttehJcodZ5Ihxufn3ulLGWWN5WGkHYm4iVSCBoGgiC0llvlqC1YOBiAmnTV4jaWgmiLMCuB5Byao5avAD1weN2xn8XaH_ADZaTsEwhI0A%3D%3D)
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

clean_body: One participant—blindfolded and hands free—set out to trial Tesla’s newest AI feature.| | | | November 27 | | | | | Trusting AI: An Unforgettable Cybertruck Trial | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. name: Jessica Mackler, EMILYs List
subject: Grateful to have you in the fight
body:
Happy Thanksgiving, Peter |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120118459/585292293/-1510725204?sourceid=1057387&refcode=WUEFEK69_20251127_JMThanksgiving_ALL_v1s4&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNWI3MDM3Ni1kY2NiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8jH0M1aGAZHUaY6IzDGc12epmgPijil4xJqSzN1ccW0=) Happy Thanksgiving, Peter.

I hope you're spending today surrounded by the people you love.

We normally wouldn't email you on Thanksgiving, but this year, we're falling behind on our November goal.

I wish I had better news to share today. But the truth is, we're facing a critical shortfall with just three days left in the month — and if we don't close this gap, we'll be forced to scale back our plans for 2026 at the exact moment we need to be ramping up.

**That's why, even on Thanksgiving, I'm reaching out to this community — because I know that when it matters most, you show up.**

Every single dollar you give today will go toward electing Democratic pro-choice women who will stand up for our values and protect our freedoms. Women who will fight to protect our reproductive rights, address our affordability crisis, and defend our very democracy.

[**So, please, will you pitch in what you can to help hit our $20,000 per day goal?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120118470/585292303/-1510725204?sourceid=1057387&refcode=WUEFEK69_20251127_JMThanksgiving_ALL_v1s4&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNWI3MDM3Ni1kY2NiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8jH0M1aGAZHUaY6IzDGc12epmgPijil4xJqSzN1ccW0=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately:_ | [DONATE $15](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120118474/585292325/-1403821653?express_lane=true&amount=15&sourceid=1057387&refcode=WUEFEK69_20251127_JMThanksgiving_ALL_v1s4&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNWI3MDM3Ni1kY2NiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8jH0M1aGAZHUaY6IzDGc12epmgPijil4xJqSzN1ccW0=) | [DONATE $35](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120118480/585292335/-1403821653?express_lane=true&amount=35&sourceid=1057387&refcode=WUEFEK69_20251127_JMThanksgiving_ALL_v1s4&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNWI3MDM3Ni1kY2NiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8jH0M1aGAZHUaY6IzDGc12epmgPijil4xJqSzN1ccW0=)

name: Voter Research // Voter Action Project
subject: Student photo IDs for voting
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
@taniel.bsky.social‬: Indiana already has stringent voter ID rules... & the GOP is advancing a bill right now to strip university IDs from the list of options you can use. This would make it much harder for students to vote. At polling site at IU Bloomington, 2/3rds of voters in November used their student IDs.
